============================================================================
  CareerTrojan — Deep Ingestion v3
  tools/ingest_deep_v3.py
============================================================================
  Plugs all coverage gaps missed by ingest_all_ai_data.py:

  Phase 1: data_cloud_solutions/ — nested schema parse
            (abbreviation_knowledge, near_not_logic, semantic_connections,
             concatenation_rules)
  Phase 2: enhanced_job_titles_database.json — sections 2-5
            (categorized_titles, career_progressions, skill_mappings,
             common_normalizations)
  Phase 3: parsed_from_automated/ — LinkedIn connections, companies,
            candidate CRM exports (custom field readers)
  Phase 4: Text glossary improvements — parenthetical abbreviations,
            numbered lists, paragraph definitions
  Phase 5: Filename-embedded company + title extraction
            from root-level interview letters / CV files
  Phase 6: Mine E:\CareerTrojan AI model artefacts
            (knowledge_graph, rule_engine, training configs,
             expert system rules, feature keyword lists)

  Output: merges new terms into collocation_data/*.json with dedup

  Usage:
    python tools/ingest_deep_v3.py                    # dry-run
    python tools/ingest_deep_v3.py --apply             # write changes
    python tools/ingest_deep_v3.py --apply --verbose   # write + detail
============================================================================
"""
import argparse
import csv
import gzip
import json
import os
import re
import sys
import time
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from datetime import date
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Set, Tuple

# --- Fix Windows console encoding -----------------------------------------
if sys.platform == "win32":
    try:
        sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
        sys.stderr.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
    except Exception:
        pass

# ── Paths -----------------------------------------------------------------
PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
AI_DATA = Path(os.environ.get(
    "CAREERTROJAN_AI_DATA",
    r" ai_data final - version\ai_data_final",
))
COLLOCATION_DIR = AI_DATA / "collocation_data"
PARENT_DIR = AI_DATA.parent   ai_data final - version
AUTOMATED_PARSER = PARENT_DIR / "automated_parser"

TODAY = date.today().isoformat()
SOURCE_TAG = "deep-ingest-v3"

# ── Noise filters (same as ingest_all_ai_data.py) -------------------------
STOPWORDS = frozenset(
    "a an the is are was were be been being have has had do does did "
    "will would shall should may might can could must need ought dare "
    "to of in for on at by with from as into through during before after "
    "above below between under over out up down off about than but or and "
    "not no nor so yet also both each either neither all any few more most "
    "other some such only own same too very just because since while if "
    "then else when where how what which who whom whose that this these "
    "those it its he him his she her they them their we us our you your "
    "i me my the per via re etc eg ie vs".split()
)

NOISE_RE = re.compile(
    r"^[\d\W]+$"            # only digits/punct
    r"|^.{0,3}$"            # too short
    r"|^.{121,}$"           # too long
    r"|[\\/<>{}|`~]"        # path/code chars
    r"|^\d"                 # leading digit
    r"|^[-+#@*]"            # leading symbol
    r"|\.com|\.co\.uk|www\." # URLs
    r"|@\w+\.\w+"           # emails
    r"|^\s*$"               # blank
)


def normalize(text: str) -> str:
    """Lowercase, strip, collapse whitespace."""
    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", text.strip().lower())


def is_valid_phrase(text: str) -> bool:
    """Check if phrase is a plausible multi-word domain term."""
    if not text or NOISE_RE.search(text):
        return False
    text = text.strip()
    words = text.split()
    if len(words) < 2 or len(words) > 8:
        return False
    stop_count = sum(1 for w in words if w.lower() in STOPWORDS)
    if stop_count > len(words) * 0.5:
        return False
    if not any(len(w) >= 3 for w in words):
        return False
    return True


def is_valid_single_or_multi(text: str) -> bool:
    """Accept single words >=4 chars OR multi-word phrases."""
    if not text or NOISE_RE.search(text):
        return False
    text = text.strip()
    words = text.split()
    if len(words) == 1:
        return len(text) >= 4 and text.isalpha()
    return is_valid_phrase(text)


def make_entry(phrase: str, label: str, confidence: float = 0.80) -> dict:
    return {
        "phrase": normalize(phrase),
        "label": label,
        "source": SOURCE_TAG,
        "confidence": confidence,
        "added_date": TODAY,
    }


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Phase 1: data_cloud_solutions/ — nested schema deep parse
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def mine_data_cloud_solutions() -> List[dict]:
    """Parse all 4 data_cloud_solutions files with schema-aware readers."""
    base = AI_DATA / "data_cloud_solutions"
    if not base.is_dir():
        return []

    entries: List[dict] = []
    seen: Set[str] = set()

    def _add(phrase: str, label: str, conf: float = 0.80):
        norm = normalize(phrase)
        if norm not in seen and is_valid_single_or_multi(norm):
            seen.add(norm)
            entries.append(make_entry(phrase, label, conf))

    # 1a. abbreviation_knowledge.json
    p = base / "abbreviation_knowledge.json"
    if p.exists():
        try:
            data = json.loads(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            ak = data if not isinstance(data, dict) else data.get("abbreviation_knowledge", data)
            # technical_abbreviations → {domain: {abbr: expansion}}
            for section_key in ("technical_abbreviations", "industry_specific"):
                section = ak.get(section_key, {})
                if isinstance(section, dict):
                    for domain, mappings in section.items():
                        if isinstance(mappings, dict):
                            for abbr, expansion in mappings.items():
                                if isinstance(expansion, str):
                                    _add(expansion, "TECH_SKILL", 0.85)
                        elif isinstance(mappings, list):
                            for item in mappings:
                                if isinstance(item, str):
                                    _add(item, "TECH_SKILL", 0.80)
            # common_variants → {term: [variants]}
            variants = ak.get("common_variants", {})
            if isinstance(variants, dict):
                for term, var_list in variants.items():
                    _add(term, "TECH_SKILL", 0.75)
                    if isinstance(var_list, list):
                        for v in var_list:
                            if isinstance(v, str):
                                _add(v, "TECH_SKILL", 0.70)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  WARN: abbreviation_knowledge.json: {e}")

    # 1b. semantic_connections.json
    p = base / "semantic_connections.json"
    if p.exists():
        try:
            data = json.loads(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            sc = data if not isinstance(data, dict) else data.get("semantic_connections", data)
            # synonyms → {group_name: [words]}
            for section in ("connection_types", "synonyms"):
                syn = sc.get(section, {})
                if isinstance(syn, dict):
                    for group, items in syn.items():
                        if isinstance(items, list):
                            for item in items:
                                if isinstance(item, str):
                                    _add(item, "SOFT_SKILL", 0.75)
                        elif isinstance(items, dict):
                            for k, v in items.items():
                                _add(k, "SOFT_SKILL", 0.75)
                                if isinstance(v, list):
                                    for vi in v:
                                        if isinstance(vi, str):
                                            _add(vi, "SOFT_SKILL", 0.75)
            # skill_hierarchies → {domain: {category: [skills]}}
            sh = sc.get("skill_hierarchies", {})
            if isinstance(sh, dict):
                for domain, cats in sh.items():
                    if isinstance(cats, dict):
                        for cat, skills in cats.items():
                            if isinstance(skills, list):
                                for s in skills:
                                    if isinstance(s, str):
                                        _add(s, "TECH_SKILL", 0.80)
            # industry_connections → {sector: [terms]}
            ic = sc.get("industry_connections", {})
            if isinstance(ic, dict):
                for sector, terms in ic.items():
                    if isinstance(terms, list):
                        for t in terms:
                            if isinstance(t, str):
                                _add(t, "INDUSTRY_TERM", 0.80)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  WARN: semantic_connections.json: {e}")

    # 1c. concatenation_rules.json
    p = base / "concatenation_rules.json"
    if p.exists():
        try:
            data = json.loads(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            cr = data if not isinstance(data, dict) else data.get("concatenation_rules", data)
            # phrase_patterns → {category: [patterns]}
            for section_key in ("phrase_patterns", "joining_rules", "compound_phrases"):
                section = cr.get(section_key, {})
                if isinstance(section, dict):
                    for cat, patterns in section.items():
                        if isinstance(patterns, list):
                            for pat in patterns:
                                if isinstance(pat, str) and not pat.startswith("{"):
                                    _add(pat, "TECH_SKILL", 0.75)
                elif isinstance(section, list):
                    for item in section:
                        if isinstance(item, str):
                            _add(item, "TECH_SKILL", 0.75)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  WARN: concatenation_rules.json: {e}")

    # 1d. near_not_logic.json
    p = base / "near_not_logic.json"
    if p.exists():
        try:
            data = json.loads(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            nn = data if not isinstance(data, dict) else data.get("near_not_logic", data)
            # context_indicators → {positive: [phrases], negative: [phrases]}
            for section_key in ("context_indicators", "exclusion_rules", "proximity_rules"):
                section = nn.get(section_key, {})
                if isinstance(section, dict):
                    for signal_type, phrases in section.items():
                        if isinstance(phrases, list):
                            for ph in phrases:
                                if isinstance(ph, str):
                                    _add(ph, "TECH_SKILL", 0.70)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  WARN: near_not_logic.json: {e}")

    return entries


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Phase 2: enhanced_job_titles_database.json — sections 2-5
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def mine_enhanced_job_titles_extended() -> List[dict]:
    """Extract from categorized_titles, career_progressions, skill_mappings."""
    p = AI_DATA / "enhanced_job_titles_database.json"
    if not p.exists():
        return []

    entries: List[dict] = []
    seen: Set[str] = set()

    def _add(phrase: str, label: str, conf: float = 0.85):
        norm = normalize(phrase)
        if norm not in seen and len(norm) >= 4:
            seen.add(norm)
            entries.append(make_entry(phrase, label, conf))

    try:
        data = json.loads(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  ERROR reading enhanced_job_titles_database.json: {e}")
        return []

    # 2a. categorized_titles → {category: [titles]}
    cat_titles = data.get("categorized_titles", {})
    if isinstance(cat_titles, dict):
        for category, titles in cat_titles.items():
            if isinstance(titles, list):
                for t in titles:
                    if isinstance(t, str):
                        _add(t, "JOB_TITLE", 0.90)
            elif isinstance(titles, dict):
                for sub, sub_titles in titles.items():
                    if isinstance(sub_titles, list):
                        for t in sub_titles:
                            if isinstance(t, str):
                                _add(t, "JOB_TITLE", 0.90)

    # 2b. career_progressions → {track: [progression_titles]}
    progr = data.get("career_progressions", {})
    if isinstance(progr, dict):
        for track, steps in progr.items():
            if isinstance(steps, list):
                for step in steps:
                    if isinstance(step, str):
                        _add(step, "JOB_TITLE", 0.85)
                    elif isinstance(step, dict):
                        for k, v in step.items():
                            if isinstance(v, str):
                                _add(v, "JOB_TITLE", 0.85)
                            _add(k, "JOB_TITLE", 0.85)

    # 2c. skill_mappings → {role: [skills]}
    sk = data.get("skill_mappings", {})
    if isinstance(sk, dict):
        for role, skills in sk.items():
            _add(role, "JOB_TITLE", 0.85)
            if isinstance(skills, list):
                for s in skills:
                    if isinstance(s, str):
                        _add(s, "TECH_SKILL", 0.80)

    # 2d. common_normalizations → {typo: correct}
    cn = data.get("common_normalizations", {})
    if isinstance(cn, dict):
        for typo, correct in cn.items():
            if isinstance(correct, str):
                _add(correct, "JOB_TITLE", 0.80)

    return entries


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Phase 3: parsed_from_automated/ — custom field readers
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def mine_parsed_from_automated() -> List[dict]:
    """Mine LinkedIn connections, companies, and contacts from parser exports."""
    base = AI_DATA / "parsed_from_automated"
    if not base.is_dir():
        return []

    entries: List[dict] = []
    seen: Set[str] = set()

    def _add(phrase: str, label: str, conf: float = 0.75):
        norm = normalize(phrase)
        if norm not in seen and len(norm) >= 4:
            seen.add(norm)
            entries.append(make_entry(phrase, label, conf))

    # 3a. LinkedIn Connections — Unnamed: 3 (Company), Unnamed: 4 (Position)
    linkedin = base / "Davids Linked in Connections - pot opps highlighted.json"
    if linkedin.exists():
        try:
            data = json.loads(linkedin.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            if isinstance(data, list):
                for row in data:
                    if not isinstance(row, dict):
                        continue
                    company = row.get("Unnamed: 3", row.get("Company", ""))
                    position = row.get("Unnamed: 4", row.get("Position", ""))
                    if isinstance(company, str) and len(company.strip()) >= 4:
                        # Don't add company names to collocations — they're not domain terms
                        # But infer industry if possible
                        pass
                    if isinstance(position, str) and is_valid_phrase(position.strip()):
                        _add(position.strip(), "JOB_TITLE", 0.70)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  WARN: LinkedIn connections: {e}")

    # 3b. companies_from_parser.json — extract company-associated terms
    companies_f = base / "companies_from_parser.json"
    if companies_f.exists():
        try:
            # Stream it — may be large
            with open(companies_f, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                raw = f.read(50_000_000)  # Read up to 50MB
            data = json.loads(raw)
            if isinstance(data, list):
                for item in data[:50000]:  # cap
                    if not isinstance(item, dict):
                        continue
                    # Try common company field names (parser uses non-standard keys)
                    for key in ("CompName", "CompName\"\"", "company_name", "name",
                                "Company", "Industry", "industry", "Sector", "sector"):
                        val = item.get(key, "")
                        if isinstance(val, str) and key.lower() in ("industry", "sector"):
                            if is_valid_phrase(val.strip()):
                                _add(val.strip(), "INDUSTRY_TERM", 0.70)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  WARN: companies_from_parser.json: {e}")

    # 3c. candidates_from_parser.json — extract job titles, skills
    candidates_f = base / "candidates_from_parser.json"
    if candidates_f.exists():
        try:
            with open(candidates_f, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                raw = f.read(50_000_000)  # Up to 50MB
            data = json.loads(raw)
            if isinstance(data, list):
                for item in data[:50000]:
                    if not isinstance(item, dict):
                        continue
                    for key in ("JobTitle", "job_title", "Position", "CurrentPosition",
                                "current_position", "Skills", "skills"):
                        val = item.get(key, "")
                        if isinstance(val, str) and is_valid_phrase(val.strip()):
                            label = "JOB_TITLE" if "title" in key.lower() or "position" in key.lower() else "TECH_SKILL"
                            _add(val.strip(), label, 0.70)
                        elif isinstance(val, list):
                            for v in val:
                                if isinstance(v, str) and is_valid_phrase(v.strip()):
                                    _add(v.strip(), "TECH_SKILL", 0.70)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  WARN: candidates_from_parser.json: {e}")

    # 3d. Notes.json and Journal.json — extract text content
    for fname, label in [("Notes.json", "INDUSTRY_TERM"), ("Journal.json", "INDUSTRY_TERM")]:
        fpath = base / fname
        if fpath.exists():
            try:
                with open(fpath, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                    raw = f.read(20_000_000)  # 20MB cap
                data = json.loads(raw)
                if isinstance(data, list):
                    for item in data[:20000]:
                        if not isinstance(item, dict):
                            continue
                        for key in ("Text", "text", "Note", "note", "Subject", "subject",
                                    "Body", "body", "Content", "content", "Description"):
                            val = item.get(key, "")
                            if isinstance(val, str):
                                _extract_terms_from_text(val, entries, seen)
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"  WARN: {fname}: {e}")

    return entries


def _extract_terms_from_text(text: str, entries: List[dict], seen: Set[str],
                             max_terms: int = 50):
    """Extract plausible multi-word terms from free text."""
    if not text or len(text) < 20:
        return
    # Split into sentences, look for capitalized phrases
    sentences = re.split(r"[.!?\n]", text)
    count = 0
    for sent in sentences:
        if count >= max_terms:
            break
        # Find capitalized multi-word sequences (likely proper nouns / titles)
        caps = re.findall(r"(?:[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+)", sent)
        for cap in caps:
            if count >= max_terms:
                break
            norm = normalize(cap)
            if norm not in seen and is_valid_phrase(norm):
                seen.add(norm)
                entries.append(make_entry(cap, "CV_SKILL", 0.65))
                count += 1


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Phase 4: Text glossary improvements
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def mine_text_glossaries_deep() -> List[dict]:
    """Improved regex patterns for the 3 glossary TXT files."""
    entries: List[dict] = []
    seen: Set[str] = set()

    files = [
        PARENT_DIR / "lists to add to the glossrys.txt",
        PARENT_DIR / "more data for glossary.txt",
        PARENT_DIR / "more data 2.txt",
    ]

    # Patterns beyond what v1 captures:
    # 1. Parenthetical abbreviations: "Advanced Product Quality Planning (APQP)"
    paren_re = re.compile(r"([A-Z][A-Za-z\s/&-]{4,60})\s*\([A-Z]{2,8}\)")
    # 2. Numbered list items: "1. Drilling Engineer" or "2) Process Safety Manager"
    numbered_re = re.compile(r"^\s*\d+[\.\)]\s+(.{4,60})\s*$")
    # 3. Colon definitions (broader): "Term: definition..."
    colon_re = re.compile(r"^([A-Za-z][A-Za-z\s/&-]{3,60})[:–—]\s")
    # 4. Dash-separated: "Term – definition..." or "Term - definition..."
    dash_re = re.compile(r"^([A-Za-z][A-Za-z\s/&\(\)]{3,60})\s+[-–—]\s")
    # 5. ALL-CAPS headings (shorter): "DRILLING FLUID" at start of line
    allcaps_re = re.compile(r"^([A-Z][A-Z\s/&-]{3,50})$")
    # 6. Inline terms in quotes or bold
    quoted_re = re.compile(r'["""]([A-Za-z][A-Za-z\s/&-]{3,50})["""]')

    def _add(phrase: str, label: str, conf: float = 0.75):
        norm = normalize(phrase)
        if norm not in seen and len(norm) >= 4:
            words = norm.split()
            if len(words) >= 2 and len(words) <= 7:
                stop_count = sum(1 for w in words if w in STOPWORDS)
                if stop_count <= len(words) * 0.5:
                    seen.add(norm)
                    entries.append(make_entry(phrase, label, conf))

    for fpath in files:
        if not fpath.exists():
            continue
        try:
            text = fpath.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
        except Exception:
            continue

        for line in text.splitlines():
            line = line.strip()
            if not line or len(line) < 5:
                continue

            # Parenthetical abbreviation pattern
            for m in paren_re.finditer(line):
                _add(m.group(1).strip(), "INDUSTRY_TERM", 0.80)

            # Numbered list items
            m = numbered_re.match(line)
            if m:
                _add(m.group(1).strip(), "INDUSTRY_TERM", 0.75)
                continue

            # ALL-CAPS heading
            m = allcaps_re.match(line)
            if m and len(m.group(1).split()) >= 2:
                _add(m.group(1).strip(), "INDUSTRY_TERM", 0.80)
                continue

            # Colon-separated term
            m = colon_re.match(line)
            if m:
                _add(m.group(1).strip(), "INDUSTRY_TERM", 0.80)

            # Dash-separated term
            m = dash_re.match(line)
            if m:
                _add(m.group(1).strip(), "INDUSTRY_TERM", 0.78)

            # Quoted terms
            for m in quoted_re.finditer(line):
                _add(m.group(1).strip(), "INDUSTRY_TERM", 0.70)

    return entries


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Phase 5: Filename-embedded company + title extraction
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def mine_filename_companies_titles() -> List[dict]:
    """Extract company names and job titles from Interview Letter filenames."""
    entries: List[dict] = []
    seen: Set[str] = set()

    def _add(phrase: str, label: str, conf: float = 0.80):
        norm = normalize(phrase)
        if norm not in seen and len(norm) >= 4:
            seen.add(norm)
            entries.append(make_entry(phrase, label, conf))

    # Pattern: "Interview Letter for COMPANY_NAME ref XXXXXX"
    interview_re = re.compile(
        r"Interview Letter for (.+?)(?:\s+ref\s+|\s*_\d{10,})", re.IGNORECASE
    )
    # Pattern: "JVC - Candidate Name" or "JVCV - Candidate Name"
    jvcv_re = re.compile(r"^JVC[V]?\s*[-–—]\s*(.+?)(?:_\d{10,}|\.json$)", re.IGNORECASE)
    # Pattern: Title-like filenames e.g. "Technical Sales Engineer - BeNeLux"
    title_re = re.compile(
        r"^\d+\s+(.+?)(?:_\d{10,}|\.json$)"
    )

    # Scan root-level JSON filenames
    companies_found = set()
    try:
        for f in AI_DATA.iterdir():
            if not f.is_file() or f.suffix.lower() != ".json":
                continue
            name = f.stem  # filename without extension

            # Interview letters → company names
            m = interview_re.search(name)
            if m:
                company = m.group(1).strip()
                # Remove "(WAS ...)" parentheticals but keep the core name
                company_clean = re.sub(r"\s*\(.*?\)\s*", " ", company).strip()
                if company_clean and len(company_clean) >= 4:
                    companies_found.add(company_clean)
                continue

            # Title-like filenames (numbered entries)
            m = title_re.match(name)
            if m:
                potential_title = m.group(1).strip()
                # Filter out candidate names (they don't have dashes typically)
                if " - " in potential_title or "Engineer" in potential_title or \
                   "Manager" in potential_title or "Director" in potential_title:
                    parts = potential_title.split(" - ")
                    for part in parts:
                        part = part.strip()
                        if is_valid_phrase(part) and any(
                            kw in part.lower() for kw in
                            ("engineer", "manager", "director", "analyst", "consultant",
                             "specialist", "officer", "architect", "designer", "lead",
                             "coordinator", "supervisor", "technician")):
                            _add(part, "JOB_TITLE", 0.75)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  WARN: filename scan: {e}")

    # Add unique company names as industry terms
    for comp in companies_found:
        # Don't add company names as collocations — but extract industry hints
        words = comp.split()
        # Only add multi-word industry-relevant suffixes
        industry_suffixes = {
            "TECHNOLOGIES", "ENGINEERING", "AUTOMATION", "SYSTEMS", "SOLUTIONS",
            "CONSULTING", "CHEMICALS", "ENERGY", "PROCESS", "CONTROLS",
        }
        for w in words:
            if w.upper() in industry_suffixes and len(words) >= 2:
                # The full company phrase is interesting as a collocation
                # but we prefer to extract the industry term embedded in it
                pass

    return entries


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Phase 6: Mine E:\CareerTrojan AI model artefacts
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def mine_ai_model_artefacts() -> List[dict]:
    """Mine knowledge graph, rule engine, feature keywords, training configs."""
    entries: List[dict] = []
    seen: Set[str] = set()

    def _add(phrase: str, label: str, conf: float = 0.80):
        norm = normalize(phrase)
        if norm not in seen and is_valid_single_or_multi(norm):
            seen.add(norm)
            entries.append(make_entry(phrase, label, conf))

    expert_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / "backend" / "ml" / "trained" / "expert"

    # 6a. Knowledge graph nodes
    kg_path = expert_dir / "knowledge_graph.json"
    if kg_path.exists():
        try:
            kg = json.loads(kg_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            nodes = kg.get("nodes", [])
            for node in nodes:
                if isinstance(node, dict):
                    name = node.get("name", node.get("label", ""))
                    if isinstance(name, str):
                        _add(name, "TECH_SKILL", 0.85)
                    # Node properties might contain skill names
                    props = node.get("properties", {})
                    if isinstance(props, dict):
                        for k, v in props.items():
                            if isinstance(v, str) and is_valid_phrase(v):
                                _add(v, "TECH_SKILL", 0.75)
                            elif isinstance(v, list):
                                for item in v:
                                    if isinstance(item, str) and is_valid_phrase(item):
                                        _add(item, "TECH_SKILL", 0.75)
            # Edges may contain relationship types
            edges = kg.get("edges", kg.get("relationships", []))
            for edge in edges:
                if isinstance(edge, dict):
                    for key in ("type", "label", "relationship"):
                        val = edge.get(key, "")
                        if isinstance(val, str) and len(val) >= 4:
                            _add(val, "TECH_SKILL", 0.70)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  WARN: knowledge_graph.json: {e}")

    # 6b. Rule engine → extract referenced skill/domain terms
    re_path = expert_dir / "rule_engine.json"
    if re_path.exists():
        try:
            rules = json.loads(re_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            if isinstance(rules, dict):
                rule_list = rules.get("rules", [])
                for rule in rule_list:
                    if isinstance(rule, dict):
                        desc = rule.get("description", "")
                        if isinstance(desc, str) and is_valid_phrase(desc):
                            _add(desc, "TECH_SKILL", 0.70)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  WARN: rule_engine.json: {e}")

    # 6c. Training data_loader.py hardcoded keywords → ensure they're in collocations
    technical_kw_multi = [
        "machine learning", "deep learning", "data science", "power bi",
        "data warehouse", "penetration testing",
    ]
    management_kw_multi = [
        "head of", "vice president",
    ]
    for kw in technical_kw_multi:
        _add(kw, "TECH_SKILL", 0.95)
    for kw in management_kw_multi:
        _add(kw, "JOB_TITLE", 0.90)

    # 6d. Collocation engine skill labels → ensure coverage
    # The collocation_engine.py defines label categories that should all have
    # representative terms
    label_categories = {
        "TECH_SKILL": "technical competency",
        "SOFT_SKILL": "interpersonal competency",
        "JOB_TITLE": "professional designation",
        "CERTIFICATION": "professional certification",
        "INDUSTRY_TERM": "sector-specific terminology",
        "METHODOLOGY": "process methodology",
        "ENGINEERING": "engineering discipline",
        "OIL_GAS": "petroleum industry",
        "MANUFACTURING": "manufacturing process",
        "FINANCIAL_SERVICES": "financial domain",
        "BIOTECH_PHARMA": "life sciences",
        "MARKETING": "marketing practice",
        "HR_RECRUITMENT": "human resources",
        "IT_CYBERSECURITY": "information security",
        "ACCOUNTING_FINANCE": "accounting practice",
        "PRODUCT_MANAGEMENT": "product development",
    }
    for label, desc in label_categories.items():
        _add(desc, label, 0.70)

    # 6e. Mine the hybrid_orchestrator_training.json for model feature names
    ho_path = PROJECT_ROOT / "backend" / "ml" / "trained" / "hybrid_orchestrator_training.json"
    if ho_path.exists():
        try:
            ho = json.loads(ho_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            # Extract any feature names or model labels
            if isinstance(ho, dict):
                for key in ("features", "labels", "categories", "dimensions"):
                    val = ho.get(key, [])
                    if isinstance(val, list):
                        for item in val:
                            if isinstance(item, str) and is_valid_phrase(item):
                                _add(item, "TECH_SKILL", 0.75)
                    elif isinstance(val, dict):
                        for k in val:
                            if isinstance(k, str) and is_valid_phrase(k):
                                _add(k, "TECH_SKILL", 0.75)
        except Exception:
            pass

    return entries


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Merge + Write Logic
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def load_collocation_file(path: Path) -> Tuple[List[dict], Set[str]]:
    """Load existing collocation JSON and return (entries, normalized_set)."""
    if not path.exists():
        return [], set()
    try:
        data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        if isinstance(data, list):
            existing_set = {normalize(e.get("phrase", "")) for e in data if "phrase" in e}
            return data, existing_set
        return [], set()
    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
        return [], set()


# Map labels to their target files
LABEL_TO_FILE = {
    "TECH_SKILL": "tech_skills.json",
    "SOFT_SKILL": "soft_skills.json",
    "JOB_TITLE": "job_titles.json",
    "CERTIFICATION": "certifications.json",
    "INDUSTRY_TERM": "industrial_terms.json",
    "METHODOLOGY": "cloud_devops.json",
    "ENGINEERING": "engineering.json",
    "OIL_GAS": "oil_gas.json",
    "MANUFACTURING": "manufacturing.json",
    "FINANCIAL_SERVICES": "financial_services.json",
    "BIOTECH_PHARMA": "biotech_pharma.json",
    "MARKETING": "marketing.json",
    "HR_RECRUITMENT": "hr_recruitment.json",
    "IT_CYBERSECURITY": "it_cybersecurity.json",
    "ACCOUNTING_FINANCE": "accounting_finance.json",
    "PRODUCT_MANAGEMENT": "product_management.json",
    "CV_SKILL": "cv_mined_phrases.json",
    "ESCO_SKILL": "tech_skills.json",
    "KNOWLEDGE": "tech_skills.json",
    "PHYSICS_ENGINEERING": "engineering.json",
}


def merge_into_collocations(
    all_entries: List[dict],
    *,
    apply: bool = False,
    verbose: bool = False,
) -> Dict[str, int]:
    """Deduplicate and merge entries into their target collocation files."""
    # Group by target file
    by_file: Dict[str, List[dict]] = defaultdict(list)
    for entry in all_entries:
        label = entry.get("label", "")
        target = LABEL_TO_FILE.get(label, "cv_mined_phrases.json")
        by_file[target].append(entry)

    stats: Dict[str, int] = {}
    total_added = 0
    total_skipped = 0

    for filename, new_entries in sorted(by_file.items()):
        path = COLLOCATION_DIR / filename
        existing, existing_set = load_collocation_file(path)
        added = 0
        skipped = 0

        for entry in new_entries:
            norm = normalize(entry.get("phrase", ""))
            if norm in existing_set:
                skipped += 1
                if verbose:
                    print(f"    SKIP (dup): {norm}")
                continue
            existing.append(entry)
            existing_set.add(norm)
            added += 1
            if verbose:
                print(f"    ADD: {norm} [{entry['label']}]")

        stats[filename] = added
        total_added += added
        total_skipped += skipped

        if apply and added > 0:
            path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                json.dump(existing, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
                f.write("\n")

        status = "WRITE" if apply and added > 0 else "dry-run"
        print(f"  {filename:35s} +{added:5d}  (skipped {skipped:5d})  [{status}]")

    return {
        "total_added": total_added,
        "total_skipped": total_skipped,
        "by_file": stats,
    }


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Logging
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def write_log(results: dict, phases: dict, elapsed: float):
    """Write run log to logs/ingest_deep_v3.log."""
    log_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / "logs"
    log_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    log_path = log_dir / "ingest_deep_v3.log"

    lines = [
        f"{'='*70}",
        f"  Deep Ingestion v3 — Run Log",
        f"  Date: {TODAY}",
        f"  Elapsed: {elapsed:.1f}s",
        f"{'='*70}",
        "",
    ]

    for phase_name, phase_data in phases.items():
        lines.append(f"  Phase: {phase_name}")
        lines.append(f"    Raw terms extracted: {phase_data['raw_count']}")
        lines.append(f"    After dedup:         {phase_data.get('dedup_count', '?')}")
        lines.append("")

    lines.append(f"  MERGE RESULTS:")
    lines.append(f"    Total new terms added:   {results['total_added']}")
    lines.append(f"    Total duplicates skipped: {results['total_skipped']}")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append(f"  BY FILE:")
    for fname, count in sorted(results.get("by_file", {}).items()):
        lines.append(f"    {fname:35s} +{count}")
    lines.append("")

    log_path.write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"\n  Log written to {log_path}")


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Update manifest
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def update_manifest(results: dict, apply: bool):
    """Update _manifest.json with deep-v3 stats."""
    manifest_path = COLLOCATION_DIR / "_manifest.json"
    if not manifest_path.exists() or not apply:
        return
    try:
        manifest = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        manifest["updated"] = TODAY
        manifest["last_deep_ingestion_v3"] = TODAY
        manifest["deep_v3_stats"] = {
            "total_new": results["total_added"],
            "total_skipped": results["total_skipped"],
            "by_file": results.get("by_file", {}),
        }
        # Update per-file counts
        for fname, added in results.get("by_file", {}).items():
            if fname in manifest.get("files", {}) and isinstance(manifest["files"][fname], dict):
                manifest["files"][fname]["deep_v3_new"] = added
                manifest["files"][fname]["last_updated"] = TODAY
                # Re-count total phrases
                fpath = COLLOCATION_DIR / fname
                if fpath.exists():
                    try:
                        data = json.loads(fpath.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
                        if isinstance(data, list):
                            manifest["files"][fname]["total_phrases"] = len(data)
                    except Exception:
                        pass
        manifest_path.write_text(
            json.dumps(manifest, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n",
            encoding="utf-8",
        )
        print(f"  Manifest updated: {manifest_path}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  WARN: manifest update failed: {e}")


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# MAIN
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Deep Ingestion v3 — plug all coverage gaps")
    parser.add_argument("--apply", action="store_true", help="Write changes (default is dry-run)")
    parser.add_argument("--verbose", "-v", action="store_true", help="Show every term")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    mode = "APPLY" if args.apply else "DRY-RUN"
    print(f"\n{'='*70}")
    print(f"  DEEP INGESTION v3 — {mode}")
    print(f"  AI Data:   {AI_DATA}")
    print(f"  Output:    {COLLOCATION_DIR}")
    print(f"  Project:   {PROJECT_ROOT}")
    print(f"{'='*70}\n")

    if not AI_DATA.exists():
        print(f"ERROR: {AI_DATA} not found")
        sys.exit(1)

    t0 = time.time()
    all_entries: List[dict] = []
    phases: Dict[str, dict] = {}

    # Phase 1: data_cloud_solutions
    print("Phase 1: data_cloud_solutions/ (nested schema parse) ...")
    p1 = mine_data_cloud_solutions()
    phases["data_cloud_solutions"] = {"raw_count": len(p1)}
    all_entries.extend(p1)
    print(f"  → {len(p1)} terms extracted\n")

    # Phase 2: enhanced_job_titles_database.json sections 2-5
    print("Phase 2: enhanced_job_titles_database.json (extended sections) ...")
    p2 = mine_enhanced_job_titles_extended()
    phases["enhanced_job_titles_ext"] = {"raw_count": len(p2)}
    all_entries.extend(p2)
    print(f"  → {len(p2)} terms extracted\n")

    # Phase 3: parsed_from_automated/
    print("Phase 3: parsed_from_automated/ (LinkedIn, companies, candidates) ...")
    p3 = mine_parsed_from_automated()
    phases["parsed_from_automated"] = {"raw_count": len(p3)}
    all_entries.extend(p3)
    print(f"  → {len(p3)} terms extracted\n")

    # Phase 4: Text glossary improvements
    print("Phase 4: Text glossary deep re-mine ...")
    p4 = mine_text_glossaries_deep()
    phases["text_glossaries_deep"] = {"raw_count": len(p4)}
    all_entries.extend(p4)
    print(f"  → {len(p4)} terms extracted\n")

    # Phase 5: Filename-embedded extraction
    print("Phase 5: Filename-embedded company/title extraction ...")
    p5 = mine_filename_companies_titles()
    phases["filename_extraction"] = {"raw_count": len(p5)}
    all_entries.extend(p5)
    print(f"  → {len(p5)} terms extracted\n")

    # Phase 6: AI model artefacts
    print("Phase 6: E:\\CareerTrojan AI model artefacts ...")
    p6 = mine_ai_model_artefacts()
    phases["ai_model_artefacts"] = {"raw_count": len(p6)}
    all_entries.extend(p6)
    print(f"  → {len(p6)} terms extracted\n")

    # Global dedup across all phases
    print(f"Total raw terms from all phases: {len(all_entries)}")
    global_seen: Set[str] = set()
    deduped: List[dict] = []
    for entry in all_entries:
        norm = normalize(entry.get("phrase", ""))
        if norm not in global_seen:
            global_seen.add(norm)
            deduped.append(entry)
    print(f"After cross-phase dedup: {len(deduped)}")
    print()

    # Merge into collocation files
    print("Merging into collocation_data files ...")
    print("-" * 70)
    results = merge_into_collocations(deduped, apply=args.apply, verbose=args.verbose)
    print("-" * 70)

    # Summary
    elapsed = time.time() - t0
    print(f"\n{'='*70}")
    print(f"  DEEP INGESTION v3 — SUMMARY")
    print(f"{'='*70}")
    print(f"  Raw terms extracted:    {len(all_entries):,}")
    print(f"  After cross-phase dedup:{len(deduped):,}")
    print(f"  New terms added:        {results['total_added']:,}")
    print(f"  Duplicates skipped:     {results['total_skipped']:,}")
    print(f"  Elapsed:                {elapsed:.1f}s")

    if not args.apply:
        print(f"\n  *** DRY RUN — no files modified ***")
        print(f"  Re-run with --apply to write changes.\n")
    else:
        update_manifest(results, args.apply)
        print(f"\n  ✓ Changes written to {COLLOCATION_DIR}\n")

    write_log(results, phases, elapsed)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
============================================================================
  CareerTrojan — Enhance Training Data
  tools/enhance_training_data.py
============================================================================
  Connects the 1.97M collocation terms to the ML training pipeline:

  1. Replaces 50 hardcoded TECHNICAL_KW with collocation-powered skill detector
  2. Derives realistic salary/match_score targets from feature data
     (replaces np.random.normal / np.random.uniform)
  3. Enriches knowledge_graph.json from collocation categories
  4. Expands case_base.json with archetype cases per industry
  5. Generates an enhanced_keywords.json that both data_loader.py
     and feature_engineering.py can import

  Usage:
    python tools/enhance_training_data.py                    # dry-run
    python tools/enhance_training_data.py --apply             # write changes
    python tools/enhance_training_data.py --apply --verbose   # detail
============================================================================
"""
import argparse
import json
import logging
import os
import re
import sys
import time
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from datetime import date, datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Set, Tuple

if sys.platform == "win32":
    try:
        sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
        sys.stderr.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
    except Exception:
        pass

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s")
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# ── Paths ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
AI_DATA = Path(os.environ.get(
    ai_data final - version\ai_data_final",
))
COLLOCATION_DIR = AI_DATA / "collocation_data"
ML_DIR = PROJECT_ROOT / "backend" / "ml"
TRAINING_DIR = ML_DIR / "training"
EXPERT_DIR = ML_DIR / "trained" / "expert"
MODELS_DIR = PROJECT_ROOT / "trained_models"
SHARED_DIR = PROJECT_ROOT / "shared"

TODAY = date.today().isoformat()


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Step 1: Build enhanced_keywords.json from collocation data
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def load_all_collocations() -> Dict[str, List[dict]]:
    """Load all collocation_data/*.json files → {label: [entries]}."""
    if not COLLOCATION_DIR.exists():
        logger.error(f"Collocation dir not found: {COLLOCATION_DIR}")
        return {}

    by_label: Dict[str, List[dict]] = defaultdict(list)
    total = 0

    for f in sorted(COLLOCATION_DIR.glob("*.json")):
        if f.name.startswith("_"):
            continue
        try:
            data = json.loads(f.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            if not isinstance(data, list):
                continue
            for entry in data:
                phrase = entry.get("phrase", "").strip().lower()
                label = entry.get("label", "UNKNOWN")
                if phrase and len(phrase) >= 3:
                    by_label[label].append(entry)
                    total += 1
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(f"  Skip {f.name}: {e}")

    logger.info(f"Loaded {total:,} collocations across {len(by_label)} labels")
    return by_label


def build_enhanced_keywords(collocations: Dict[str, List[dict]]) -> dict:
    """Build a structured keywords file from collocations for ML use."""

    # Map collocation labels to training keyword categories
    LABEL_MAP = {
        # Technical skills
        "TECH_SKILL": "technical",
        "IT_CYBERSECURITY": "technical",
        "ENGINEERING": "technical",
        "METHODOLOGY": "technical",
        "PRODUCT_MANAGEMENT": "technical",
        # Industry terms
        "INDUSTRY_TERM": "industry",
        "OIL_GAS": "industry",
        "MANUFACTURING": "industry",
        "FINANCIAL_SERVICES": "industry",
        "BIOTECH_PHARMA": "industry",
        "ACCOUNTING_FINANCE": "industry",
        "MARKETING": "industry",
        # Soft skills
        "SOFT_SKILL": "management",
        "HR_RECRUITMENT": "management",
        # Job titles
        "JOB_TITLE": "job_titles",
        # Certifications
        "CERTIFICATION": "certifications",
        # Other
        "CV_SKILL": "technical",
        "ESCO_SKILL": "technical",
        "KNOWLEDGE": "technical",
    }

    categories: Dict[str, Set[str]] = defaultdict(set)
    industry_terms: Dict[str, Set[str]] = defaultdict(set)

    for label, entries in collocations.items():
        cat = LABEL_MAP.get(label, "technical")
        for entry in entries:
            phrase = entry.get("phrase", "").strip().lower()
            conf = entry.get("confidence", 0.5)
            if len(phrase) >= 3 and conf >= 0.5:
                categories[cat].add(phrase)

                # Also track by specific industry label
                if label in ("OIL_GAS", "MANUFACTURING", "FINANCIAL_SERVICES",
                             "BIOTECH_PHARMA", "MARKETING", "ACCOUNTING_FINANCE"):
                    industry_terms[label].add(phrase)

    # Build the output structure
    output = {
        "metadata": {
            "generated": TODAY,
            "source": "collocation_data",
            "purpose": "Enhanced keyword sets for ML training pipeline",
        },
        "technical_keywords": sorted(categories.get("technical", set())),
        "management_keywords": sorted(categories.get("management", set())),
        "industry_keywords": sorted(categories.get("industry", set())),
        "job_title_keywords": sorted(categories.get("job_titles", set())),
        "certification_keywords": sorted(categories.get("certifications", set())),
        "industry_specific": {},
        "stats": {
            "total_keywords": sum(len(v) for v in categories.values()),
            "technical_count": len(categories.get("technical", set())),
            "management_count": len(categories.get("management", set())),
            "industry_count": len(categories.get("industry", set())),
            "job_titles_count": len(categories.get("job_titles", set())),
            "certifications_count": len(categories.get("certifications", set())),
        },
    }

    # Add industry-specific keyword sets
    for ind_label, phrases in sorted(industry_terms.items()):
        output["industry_specific"][ind_label] = sorted(phrases)

    return output


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Step 2: Derive realistic salary/match targets
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def generate_salary_derivation_code() -> str:
    """Generate Python code snippet to replace synthetic salary targets."""
    return '''
def derive_salary_estimate(row: dict) -> float:
    """
    Derive a realistic salary estimate from features instead of random noise.
    Based on UK recruitment market heuristics.
    """
    base = 25000.0  # UK baseline

    # Experience premium: ~+3000/yr for first 10 years, ~+2000/yr after
    years = row.get("years_experience", 0)
    if years <= 10:
        base += years * 3000
    else:
        base += 30000 + (years - 10) * 2000

    # Education premium
    edu = row.get("education_level", 0)
    base += edu * 3000  # PhD=5 → +15000, Masters=4 → +12000, etc.

    # Technical skills premium
    skills = row.get("skills_count", 0)
    base += min(skills, 20) * 500  # cap at 20 skills

    # Management premium
    if row.get("has_management", 0):
        base *= 1.20

    # Industry premium (some sectors pay more)
    industry = row.get("industry", "").lower()
    industry_mult = {
        "finance": 1.30, "technology": 1.25, "oil & gas": 1.35,
        "pharmaceutical": 1.20, "consulting": 1.25, "aerospace & defence": 1.15,
    }
    base *= industry_mult.get(industry, 1.0)

    return round(min(max(base, 20000), 200000), 0)


def derive_match_score(row: dict) -> float:
    """
    Derive a job match score from features instead of random uniform.
    Range: 0.0 - 1.0
    """
    score = 0.0
    weights = {
        "skills_count": (0.20, lambda x: min(x / 15.0, 1.0)),
        "years_experience": (0.15, lambda x: min(x / 15.0, 1.0)),
        "education_level": (0.10, lambda x: min(x / 5.0, 1.0)),
        "has_technical": (0.15, lambda x: float(x)),
        "has_management": (0.10, lambda x: float(x)),
        "text_length": (0.10, lambda x: min(x / 5000.0, 1.0)),
        "companies_count": (0.05, lambda x: min(x / 5.0, 1.0)),
    }
    for key, (weight, normalizer) in weights.items():
        val = row.get(key, 0)
        score += weight * normalizer(val)

    # Baseline: even candidates with no features get 0.15
    score = 0.15 + score * 0.80
    return round(min(max(score, 0.05), 0.99), 4)
'''


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Step 3: Enrich knowledge graph from collocations
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def enrich_knowledge_graph(collocations: Dict[str, List[dict]]) -> dict:
    """Build an enriched knowledge graph from collocation data."""
    existing_path = EXPERT_DIR / "knowledge_graph.json"
    if existing_path.exists():
        try:
            kg = json.loads(existing_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        except Exception:
            kg = {"metadata": {}, "nodes": [], "edges": []}
    else:
        kg = {"metadata": {}, "nodes": [], "edges": []}

    existing_ids = {n.get("id") for n in kg.get("nodes", [])}
    existing_edges = {
        (e["source"], e["target"], e["relation"])
        for e in kg.get("edges", [])
    }

    new_nodes = []
    new_edges = []

    # Add category nodes (one per collocation label)
    category_nodes = {
        "TECH_SKILL": ("Technical_Skills", "skill_category"),
        "SOFT_SKILL": ("Soft_Skills", "skill_category"),
        "JOB_TITLE": ("Job_Titles", "entity_category"),
        "CERTIFICATION": ("Certifications", "credential_category"),
        "INDUSTRY_TERM": ("Industry_Terms", "domain_category"),
        "OIL_GAS": ("Oil_Gas_Domain", "industry"),
        "MANUFACTURING": ("Manufacturing_Domain", "industry"),
        "FINANCIAL_SERVICES": ("Financial_Services_Domain", "industry"),
        "BIOTECH_PHARMA": ("Biotech_Pharma_Domain", "industry"),
        "ENGINEERING": ("Engineering_Domain", "industry"),
        "IT_CYBERSECURITY": ("IT_Cybersecurity_Domain", "industry"),
        "MARKETING": ("Marketing_Domain", "industry"),
        "ACCOUNTING_FINANCE": ("Accounting_Finance_Domain", "industry"),
        "HR_RECRUITMENT": ("HR_Recruitment_Domain", "industry"),
        "PRODUCT_MANAGEMENT": ("Product_Management_Domain", "industry"),
        "METHODOLOGY": ("Methodology_Domain", "methodology"),
    }

    for label, (node_id, node_type) in category_nodes.items():
        if node_id not in existing_ids:
            new_nodes.append({"id": node_id, "type": node_type})
            existing_ids.add(node_id)

        # Connect to parent category
        if node_type == "industry":
            edge = (node_id, "Industry_Terms", "belongs_to")
            if edge not in existing_edges:
                # Ensure Industry_Terms exists
                if "Industry_Terms" not in existing_ids:
                    new_nodes.append({"id": "Industry_Terms", "type": "domain_category"})
                    existing_ids.add("Industry_Terms")
                new_edges.append({"source": node_id, "target": "Industry_Terms", "relation": "belongs_to"})
                existing_edges.add(edge)

    # Add top skill/term nodes (top 50 per category by confidence)
    MAX_SKILLS_PER_CATEGORY = 50

    for label, entries in collocations.items():
        node_id = category_nodes.get(label, (None, None))[0]
        if not node_id:
            continue

        # Sort by confidence descending, take top N
        sorted_entries = sorted(entries, key=lambda e: e.get("confidence", 0), reverse=True)
        for entry in sorted_entries[:MAX_SKILLS_PER_CATEGORY]:
            phrase = entry.get("phrase", "").strip()
            if not phrase or len(phrase) < 3:
                continue
            # Create a sanitized node ID
            skill_id = re.sub(r"[^a-zA-Z0-9_]", "_", phrase.title().replace(" ", "_"))
            if not skill_id or skill_id in existing_ids:
                continue
            if len(skill_id) > 60:
                continue

            skill_type = {
                "TECH_SKILL": "technical_skill",
                "SOFT_SKILL": "soft_skill",
                "JOB_TITLE": "job_title",
                "CERTIFICATION": "certification",
                "INDUSTRY_TERM": "industry_term",
            }.get(label, "domain_term")

            new_nodes.append({
                "id": skill_id,
                "type": skill_type,
                "name": phrase,
                "confidence": entry.get("confidence", 0.5),
            })
            existing_ids.add(skill_id)

            # Connect to category
            edge = (node_id, skill_id, "contains")
            if edge not in existing_edges:
                new_edges.append({"source": node_id, "target": skill_id, "relation": "contains"})
                existing_edges.add(edge)

    # Merge into knowledge graph
    kg["nodes"].extend(new_nodes)
    kg["edges"].extend(new_edges)
    kg["metadata"]["version"] = "3.0"
    kg["metadata"]["enriched_date"] = TODAY
    kg["metadata"]["total_nodes"] = len(kg["nodes"])
    kg["metadata"]["total_edges"] = len(kg["edges"])
    kg["metadata"]["collocation_labels_used"] = len(category_nodes)

    return kg


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Step 4: Expand case base with industry archetypes
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def expand_case_base(collocations: Dict[str, List[dict]]) -> dict:
    """Build expanded case_base.json with industry archetypes."""
    existing_path = EXPERT_DIR / "case_base.json"
    if existing_path.exists():
        try:
            cb = json.loads(existing_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
        except Exception:
            cb = {"metadata": {}, "cases": []}
    else:
        cb = {"metadata": {}, "cases": []}

    existing_ids = {c.get("case_id") for c in cb.get("cases", [])}

    # Build industry archetypes from collocations
    INDUSTRY_ARCHETYPES = {
        "OIL_GAS": {
            "industry": "Oil & Gas",
            "seniority_levels": ["junior", "mid", "senior"],
            "typical_skills": 8,
            "typical_years": {"junior": 2, "mid": 6, "senior": 15},
            "salary_range": {"junior": 35000, "mid": 55000, "senior": 90000},
        },
        "MANUFACTURING": {
            "industry": "Manufacturing",
            "seniority_levels": ["junior", "mid", "senior"],
            "typical_skills": 6,
            "typical_years": {"junior": 1, "mid": 5, "senior": 12},
            "salary_range": {"junior": 28000, "mid": 42000, "senior": 70000},
        },
        "FINANCIAL_SERVICES": {
            "industry": "Financial Services",
            "seniority_levels": ["junior", "mid", "senior"],
            "typical_skills": 7,
            "typical_years": {"junior": 1, "mid": 4, "senior": 10},
            "salary_range": {"junior": 35000, "mid": 60000, "senior": 110000},
        },
        "BIOTECH_PHARMA": {
            "industry": "Biotech & Pharma",
            "seniority_levels": ["junior", "mid", "senior"],
            "typical_skills": 9,
            "typical_years": {"junior": 2, "mid": 5, "senior": 12},
            "salary_range": {"junior": 32000, "mid": 50000, "senior": 85000},
        },
        "IT_CYBERSECURITY": {
            "industry": "IT & Cybersecurity",
            "seniority_levels": ["junior", "mid", "senior"],
            "typical_skills": 10,
            "typical_years": {"junior": 1, "mid": 4, "senior": 10},
            "salary_range": {"junior": 30000, "mid": 50000, "senior": 85000},
        },
        "ENGINEERING": {
            "industry": "Engineering",
            "seniority_levels": ["junior", "mid", "senior"],
            "typical_skills": 7,
            "typical_years": {"junior": 2, "mid": 6, "senior": 15},
            "salary_range": {"junior": 30000, "mid": 45000, "senior": 75000},
        },
        "MARKETING": {
            "industry": "Marketing & Communications",
            "seniority_levels": ["junior", "mid", "senior"],
            "typical_skills": 6,
            "typical_years": {"junior": 1, "mid": 4, "senior": 10},
            "salary_range": {"junior": 25000, "mid": 40000, "senior": 70000},
        },
        "ACCOUNTING_FINANCE": {
            "industry": "Accounting & Finance",
            "seniority_levels": ["junior", "mid", "senior"],
            "typical_skills": 6,
            "typical_years": {"junior": 1, "mid": 5, "senior": 12},
            "salary_range": {"junior": 28000, "mid": 48000, "senior": 80000},
        },
    }

    new_cases = []
    case_counter = len(existing_ids)

    for label, archetype in INDUSTRY_ARCHETYPES.items():
        # Get top skills from this industry's collocations
        industry_skills = []
        entries = collocations.get(label, [])
        sorted_entries = sorted(entries, key=lambda e: e.get("confidence", 0), reverse=True)
        for entry in sorted_entries[:15]:
            phrase = entry.get("phrase", "").strip()
            if phrase and len(phrase) >= 3:
                industry_skills.append(phrase)

        # Also pull in relevant TECH_SKILL terms
        tech_entries = collocations.get("TECH_SKILL", [])
        # Filter for terms that overlap with this industry
        industry_kw = archetype["industry"].lower().split()
        relevant_tech = []
        for entry in tech_entries:
            phrase = entry.get("phrase", "").strip().lower()
            if any(kw in phrase for kw in industry_kw):
                relevant_tech.append(phrase)
            if len(relevant_tech) >= 10:
                break

        combined_skills = list(set(industry_skills + relevant_tech))[:20]

        for seniority in archetype["seniority_levels"]:
            case_id = f"archetype_{label.lower()}_{seniority}"
            if case_id in existing_ids:
                continue

            years = archetype["typical_years"][seniority]
            salary = archetype["salary_range"][seniority]
            skills_count = archetype["typical_skills"]

            # Select appropriate subset of skills
            if seniority == "junior":
                case_skills = combined_skills[:max(3, skills_count - 2)]
            elif seniority == "mid":
                case_skills = combined_skills[:skills_count]
            else:
                case_skills = combined_skills[:min(len(combined_skills), skills_count + 4)]

            case_counter += 1
            new_cases.append({
                "case_id": case_id,
                "description": f"{seniority.title()} {archetype['industry']} professional",
                "industry": archetype["industry"],
                "seniority": seniority,
                "seniority_label": {"junior": 0, "mid": 1, "senior": 2}[seniority],
                "experience_years": years,
                "salary_estimate": salary,
                "skills_count": len(case_skills),
                "skills": case_skills,
                "has_technical": True,
                "has_management": seniority in ("mid", "senior"),
                "has_degree": seniority != "junior",
                "match_score_hint": {"junior": 0.45, "mid": 0.65, "senior": 0.85}[seniority],
                "added_date": TODAY,
                "source": "collocation_archetype",
            })

    cb["cases"].extend(new_cases)
    cb["metadata"]["version"] = "2.0"
    cb["metadata"]["enriched_date"] = TODAY
    cb["metadata"]["total_cases"] = len(cb["cases"])
    cb["metadata"]["industry_archetypes"] = len(INDUSTRY_ARCHETYPES)

    return cb


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Step 5: Generate enhanced data_loader.py patch
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def generate_data_loader_patch() -> str:
    """Generate instructions for patching data_loader.py."""
    return """
# -----------------------------------------------------------------
# PATCH: backend/ml/training/data_loader.py
# Replace the hardcoded TECHNICAL_KW with collocation-powered lookup
# -----------------------------------------------------------------
# At the top of the file, add:

import os

def _load_enhanced_keywords() -> dict:
    \"\"\"Load enhanced keywords from shared/enhanced_keywords.json.\"\"\"
    kw_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent.parent / "shared" / "enhanced_keywords.json"
    if kw_path.exists():
        try:
            data = json.loads(kw_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            return {
                "technical": set(data.get("technical_keywords", [])),
                "management": set(data.get("management_keywords", [])),
                "industry": set(data.get("industry_keywords", [])),
                "job_titles": set(data.get("job_title_keywords", [])),
                "certifications": set(data.get("certification_keywords", [])),
            }
        except Exception:
            pass
    return None

_ENHANCED_KW = _load_enhanced_keywords()

# Then replace TECHNICAL_KW with:
TECHNICAL_KW = _ENHANCED_KW["technical"] if _ENHANCED_KW else {
    # ... original 50 keywords as fallback ...
}

MANAGEMENT_KW = _ENHANCED_KW["management"] if _ENHANCED_KW else {
    # ... original management keywords as fallback ...
}

# This gives you ~500K+ technical keywords instead of 50!
"""


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Step 6: Patch train_all_models.py to use derived targets
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def generate_train_all_models_patch() -> str:
    """Generate the code to replace synthetic targets."""
    return '''
# In run_statistical_training(), replace lines 144-145:
#   "salary_expectation": np.random.normal(50000, 15000, len(df)).clip(20000, 150000),
#   "job_match_score": np.random.uniform(0.3, 0.95, len(df)),
# With:

def _derive_salary(row):
    """Feature-based salary estimate (UK market heuristics)."""
    base = 25000.0
    years = row.get("years_experience", 0)
    base += min(years, 10) * 3000 + max(0, years - 10) * 2000
    base += row.get("education_level", 0) * 3000
    base += min(row.get("skills_count", 0), 20) * 500
    if row.get("has_management", 0):
        base *= 1.20
    industry_mult = {
        "Finance": 1.30, "Technology": 1.25, "Oil & Gas": 1.35,
        "Pharmaceutical": 1.20, "Consulting": 1.25,
    }
    base *= industry_mult.get(row.get("industry", ""), 1.0)
    return round(min(max(base, 20000), 200000), 0)

def _derive_match_score(row):
    """Feature-based match score."""
    score = 0.15
    score += 0.20 * min(row.get("skills_count", 0) / 15.0, 1.0)
    score += 0.15 * min(row.get("years_experience", 0) / 15.0, 1.0)
    score += 0.10 * min(row.get("education_level", 0) / 5.0, 1.0)
    score += 0.15 * float(row.get("has_technical", 0))
    score += 0.10 * float(row.get("has_management", 0))
    score += 0.10 * min(row.get("text_length", 0) / 5000.0, 1.0)
    return round(min(max(score, 0.05), 0.99), 4)

# Then in run_statistical_training:
train_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "years_experience": df["experience_years_est"],
    "skills_count": df["skills_count"],
    "salary_expectation": df.apply(lambda r: _derive_salary({
        "years_experience": r["experience_years_est"],
        "education_level": r["highest_degree_rank"],
        "skills_count": r["skills_count"],
        "has_management": r["has_management_keywords"],
        "industry": r["industry_hint"],
    }), axis=1),
    "job_match_score": df.apply(lambda r: _derive_match_score({
        "skills_count": r["skills_count"],
        "years_experience": r["experience_years_est"],
        "education_level": r["highest_degree_rank"],
        "has_technical": r["has_technical_skills"],
        "has_management": r["has_management_keywords"],
        "text_length": r["text_length"],
    }), axis=1),
    ...  # rest unchanged
})
'''


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# MAIN
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Enhance training data with collocation terms")
    parser.add_argument("--apply", action="store_true", help="Write changes")
    parser.add_argument("--verbose", "-v", action="store_true", help="Verbose output")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    mode = "APPLY" if args.apply else "DRY-RUN"
    print(f"\n{'='*70}")
    print(f"  ENHANCE TRAINING DATA — {mode}")
    print(f"  Collocation Dir: {COLLOCATION_DIR}")
    print(f"  ML Dir:          {ML_DIR}")
    print(f"  Expert Dir:      {EXPERT_DIR}")
    print(f"{'='*70}\n")

    t0 = time.time()

    # Step 1: Load collocations and build enhanced keywords
    print("Step 1: Loading collocations and building enhanced keywords ...")
    collocations = load_all_collocations()
    if not collocations:
        print("ERROR: No collocations loaded — check path")
        sys.exit(1)

    enhanced_kw = build_enhanced_keywords(collocations)
    total_kw = enhanced_kw["stats"]["total_keywords"]
    print(f"  Technical keywords: {enhanced_kw['stats']['technical_count']:,}")
    print(f"  Management keywords: {enhanced_kw['stats']['management_count']:,}")
    print(f"  Industry keywords: {enhanced_kw['stats']['industry_count']:,}")
    print(f"  Job title keywords: {enhanced_kw['stats']['job_titles_count']:,}")
    print(f"  Certification keywords: {enhanced_kw['stats']['certifications_count']:,}")
    print(f"  TOTAL: {total_kw:,} keywords (vs 50 hardcoded)")
    print()

    # Step 2: Enrich knowledge graph
    print("Step 2: Enriching knowledge graph ...")
    kg = enrich_knowledge_graph(collocations)
    print(f"  Total nodes: {kg['metadata']['total_nodes']}")
    print(f"  Total edges: {kg['metadata']['total_edges']}")
    print()

    # Step 3: Expand case base
    print("Step 3: Expanding case base with industry archetypes ...")
    cb = expand_case_base(collocations)
    print(f"  Total cases: {cb['metadata']['total_cases']}")
    print(f"  Industry archetypes: {cb['metadata'].get('industry_archetypes', 0)}")
    print()

    if args.apply:
        # Write enhanced_keywords.json
        SHARED_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        kw_path = SHARED_DIR / "enhanced_keywords.json"
        with open(kw_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            json.dump(enhanced_kw, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
            f.write("\n")
        print(f"  WRITTEN: {kw_path}")
        print(f"           ({total_kw:,} keywords)")

        # Write enriched knowledge graph
        EXPERT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        kg_path = EXPERT_DIR / "knowledge_graph.json"
        with open(kg_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            json.dump(kg, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
            f.write("\n")
        print(f"  WRITTEN: {kg_path}")
        print(f"           ({kg['metadata']['total_nodes']} nodes, {kg['metadata']['total_edges']} edges)")

        # Write expanded case base
        cb_path = EXPERT_DIR / "case_base.json"
        with open(cb_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            json.dump(cb, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
            f.write("\n")
        print(f"  WRITTEN: {cb_path}")
        print(f"           ({cb['metadata']['total_cases']} cases)")

        # Write salary derivation functions to shared module
        salary_path = SHARED_DIR / "salary_derivation.py"
        with open(salary_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            f.write('"""Auto-generated salary & match derivation functions."""\n\n')
            f.write(generate_salary_derivation_code())
            f.write("\n")
        print(f"  WRITTEN: {salary_path}")

    else:
        print("  DRY-RUN — no files written")

    # Summary
    elapsed = time.time() - t0
    print(f"\n{'='*70}")
    print(f"  ENHANCEMENT COMPLETE — {elapsed:.1f}s")
    print(f"{'='*70}")
    print(f"  Enhanced keywords:     {total_kw:,} (was 50 hardcoded)")
    print(f"  Knowledge graph nodes: {kg['metadata']['total_nodes']} (was 12)")
    print(f"  Knowledge graph edges: {kg['metadata']['total_edges']} (was ~10)")
    print(f"  Case base entries:     {cb['metadata']['total_cases']} (was 3)")

    if not args.apply:
        print(f"\n  *** DRY RUN — re-run with --apply to write changes ***")

    print()
    print("  NEXT STEPS (manual patches needed):")
    print("  1. Patch data_loader.py to use shared/enhanced_keywords.json")
    print("  2. Patch train_all_models.py to use derived salary/match targets")
    print("  3. Re-run: python backend/ml/training/train_all_models.py")
    print()

    # Write log
    log_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / "logs"
    log_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    log_path = log_dir / "enhance_training_data.log"
    log_lines = [
        f"{'='*70}",
        f"  Enhance Training Data — Run Log",
        f"  Date: {TODAY}  Mode: {mode}  Elapsed: {elapsed:.1f}s",
        f"{'='*70}",
        f"  Enhanced keywords:     {total_kw:,}",
        f"  Knowledge graph nodes: {kg['metadata']['total_nodes']}",
        f"  Knowledge graph edges: {kg['metadata']['total_edges']}",
        f"  Case base entries:     {cb['metadata']['total_cases']}",
        "",
    ]
    log_path.write_text("\n".join(log_lines), encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"  Log: {log_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

Consoodation of data #!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
consolidate_ai_data.py — Merge per-record JSON files into JSONL + gzip bundles.
============================================================================
Targets the ai_data_final directory where ~442K individual tiny JSONs bloat the
filesystem.  Each target subfolder is consolidated into:
    <folder>_consolidated.jsonl.gz     (one JSON object per line, gzip)
    <folder>_manifest.json             (counts, checksums, timestamps)

Usage:
    python tools/consolidate_ai_data.py                  # dry-run report
    python tools/consolidate_ai_data.py --apply          # do the consolidation
    python tools/consolidate_ai_data.py --apply --purge  # … and delete originals

Safety:
- Dry-run is the default — prints what it would do.
- The manifest records every original filename so data is traceable.
- Originals are NOT deleted unless --purge is explicitly given.
- Files that fail to parse are logged but don't block execution.
"""

import argparse, gzip, hashlib, json, os, sys, time
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path

AI_DATA_ROOT = Path(r"L:\VS ai_data final - version\ai_data_final")

# ── Folders to consolidate ──────────────────────────────────────────
# Each tuple: (subfolder_name, min_files_to_trigger, description)
TARGETS = [
    ("journal_entries",  100, "Journal entry stubs from CRM export"),
    ("profiles",         100, "Candidate profile records"),
    ("notes",            100, "CRM note stubs"),
    ("normalized",       100, "Normalised/parsed CV text"),
    ("cv_files",         100, "CV file metadata + extracted data"),
    ("contacts",         100, "Contact records"),
    ("job_matches",       10, "Job match records"),
    ("parsed_resumes",    10, "Parsed resume extractions"),
    ("job_titles",        10, "Job title reference data"),
    ("email_extracted",   10, "Extracted email content"),
]

# Root-level loose JSONs are also a target
ROOT_LOOSE_LABEL = "_root_level_documents"


def md5_file(path: Path) -> str:
    h = hashlib.md5()
    with open(path, "rb") as f:
        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(8192), b""):
            h.update(chunk)
    return h.hexdigest()


def consolidate_folder(
    folder_path: Path,
    label: str,
    description: str,
    apply: bool,
    purge: bool,
) -> dict:
    """Consolidate all individual JSONs in *folder_path* into a single .jsonl.gz."""
    json_files = sorted(
        [f for f in folder_path.iterdir() if f.is_file() and f.suffix.lower() == ".json"],
        key=lambda p: p.name,
    )
    if not json_files:
        return {"label": label, "status": "skipped", "reason": "no JSON files"}

    stats = {
        "label": label,
        "description": description,
        "source_dir": str(folder_path),
        "original_count": len(json_files),
        "original_size_bytes": sum(f.stat().st_size for f in json_files),
        "errors": [],
        "empty_records": 0,
        "non_empty_records": 0,
    }

    out_jsonl = folder_path / f"{label}_consolidated.jsonl.gz"
    out_manifest = folder_path / f"{label}_manifest.json"

    if not apply:
        stats["status"] = "dry-run"
        stats["would_create"] = str(out_jsonl)
        # Quick scan for empty records
        sample_size = min(200, len(json_files))
        empty = 0
        for jf in json_files[:sample_size]:
            try:
                data = json.loads(jf.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
                if isinstance(data, dict):
                    # Empty if all string values are blank/None
                    has_content = any(
                        v not in (None, "", [], {})
                        for k, v in data.items()
                        if k not in ("source", "processed_date", "source_file")
                    )
                    if not has_content:
                        empty += 1
            except Exception:
                pass
        stats["empty_sample_pct"] = round(empty / sample_size * 100, 1) if sample_size else 0
        return stats

    # ── Apply mode ──
    print(f"  Consolidating {label}/ — {len(json_files):,} files …", flush=True)
    manifest_entries = []
    written = 0
    t0 = time.time()

    with gzip.open(out_jsonl, "wt", encoding="utf-8", compresslevel=6) as gz:
        for jf in json_files:
            try:
                raw = jf.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
                data = json.loads(raw)
                # Tag with original filename for traceability
                if isinstance(data, dict):
                    data["__source_file"] = jf.name
                    # Check emptiness
                    has_content = any(
                        v not in (None, "", [], {})
                        for k, v in data.items()
                        if k not in ("source", "processed_date", "source_file", "__source_file")
                    )
                    if has_content:
                        stats["non_empty_records"] += 1
                    else:
                        stats["empty_records"] += 1
                gz.write(json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
                manifest_entries.append(jf.name)
                written += 1
            except Exception as e:
                stats["errors"].append({"file": jf.name, "error": str(e)})

    elapsed = time.time() - t0
    compressed_size = out_jsonl.stat().st_size

    stats.update({
        "status": "consolidated",
        "written": written,
        "compressed_file": str(out_jsonl),
        "compressed_size_bytes": compressed_size,
        "compression_ratio": round(stats["original_size_bytes"] / compressed_size, 1) if compressed_size else 0,
        "elapsed_seconds": round(elapsed, 1),
    })

    # Write manifest
    manifest = {
        "label": label,
        "description": description,
        "consolidated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "original_count": len(json_files),
        "written": written,
        "errors": len(stats["errors"]),
        "empty_records": stats["empty_records"],
        "non_empty_records": stats["non_empty_records"],
        "original_size_bytes": stats["original_size_bytes"],
        "compressed_size_bytes": compressed_size,
        "compression_ratio": stats.get("compression_ratio", 0),
        "files": manifest_entries,
    }
    out_manifest.write_text(json.dumps(manifest, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8")

    # Purge originals if requested
    if purge:
        deleted = 0
        for jf in json_files:
            if jf.name.endswith("_consolidated.jsonl.gz") or jf.name.endswith("_manifest.json"):
                continue
            try:
                jf.unlink()
                deleted += 1
            except Exception as e:
                stats["errors"].append({"file": jf.name, "error": f"delete failed: {e}"})
        stats["purged"] = deleted
        print(f"    Purged {deleted:,} original files")
