def classify_term(text: str) -> str:
    """Auto-classify a term into the best matching category."""
    text_lower = text.lower()
    scores = {}
    for category, keywords in CATEGORY_KEYWORDS.items():
        score = sum(1 for kw in keywords if kw in text_lower)
        if score > 0:
            scores[category] = score
    if scores:
        return max(scores, key=scores.get)
    return "INDUSTRIAL_SKILL"  # default fallback

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#  DATA MINERS — one per folder/source type
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class IngestionStats:
    """Track ingestion statistics."""
    def __init__(self):
        self.files_scanned = 0
        self.files_with_data = 0
        self.terms_extracted = Counter()  # category → count
        self.errors = 0
        self.skipped_folders = []

    def report(self) -> str:
        lines = [
            "\n═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
            "  INGESTION STATISTICS",
            "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
            f"  Files scanned:    {self.files_scanned:,}",
            f"  Files with data:  {self.files_with_data:,}",
            f"  Errors:           {self.errors:,}",
            f"  Total terms:      {sum(self.terms_extracted.values()):,}",
            "",
            "  Terms by category:",
        ]
        for cat, count in sorted(self.terms_extracted.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
            lines.append(f"    {cat:30s} {count:>8,}")
        if self.skipped_folders:
            lines.append(f"\n  Skipped folders: {', '.join(self.skipped_folders)}")
        lines.append("═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
        return "\n".join(lines)


def mine_contacts(verbose: bool = False) -> List[dict]:
    """Extract job titles and company names from contacts/ folder."""
    folder = AI_DATA / "contacts"
    if not folder.exists():
        return []

    entries = []
    seen = set()
    count = 0

    for fp in folder.glob("*.json"):
        data = load_json(fp)
        if not data or not isinstance(data, dict):
            continue
        count += 1

        # Extract job title
        title = data.get("title", "").strip()
        if title and normalize(title) not in seen:
            if is_valid_multiword(title) or (is_valid_single_term(title) and len(title.split()) >= 2):
                seen.add(normalize(title))
                entries.append(make_entry(title, "JOB_TITLE", 0.75))

        # Extract company name (for company collocation)
        company = data.get("company", "").strip()
        if company and len(company) > 3 and normalize(company) not in seen:
            # We don't store raw company names as collocations, but we can
            # extract multi-word role descriptors from them
            pass

    if verbose:
        print(f"  contacts/: scanned {count:,} files → {len(entries):,} job titles")
    return entries


def mine_profiles(verbose: bool = False) -> List[dict]:
    """Extract terms from profiles/ (candidate records with career summaries)."""
    folder = AI_DATA / "profiles"
    if not folder.exists():
        return []

    entries = []
    seen = set()
    count = 0
    batch_size = 500  # process in batches for memory efficiency

    for fp in folder.glob("*.json"):
        data = load_json(fp)
        if not data or not isinstance(data, dict):
            continue
        count += 1

        # Extract job titles from Career Summary
        career = data.get("Career Summary", "")
        quals = data.get("Qualifications", "")
        comments = data.get("Comments", "")
        full_text = f"{career} {quals} {comments}"

        # Extract multi-word terms using simple NLP heuristics
        extracted = extract_terms_from_text(full_text)
        for phrase, label in extracted:
            nkey = normalize(phrase)
            if nkey not in seen:
                seen.add(nkey)
                entries.append(make_entry(phrase, label, 0.70))

        # Periodic progress
        if verbose and count % 5000 == 0:
            print(f"    profiles/: processed {count:,} files, {len(entries):,} terms so far...")

    if verbose:
        print(f"  profiles/: scanned {count:,} files → {len(entries):,} terms")
    return entries


def mine_job_titles_folder(verbose: bool = False) -> List[dict]:
    """Extract from job_titles/ folder (structured title→category files)."""
    folder = AI_DATA / "job_titles"
    if not folder.exists():
        return []

    entries = []
    seen = set()

    # Individual job title files
    for fp in folder.glob("*.json"):
        if fp.name == "job_titles_master.json":
            continue
        data = load_json(fp)
        if not data or not isinstance(data, dict):
            continue
        title = data.get("title", "").strip()
        category = data.get("category", "").strip()
        if title:
            nkey = normalize(title)
            if nkey not in seen:
                seen.add(nkey)
                entries.append(make_entry(title, "JOB_TITLE", 0.90))
            # Also store the category as a term if multi-word
            if category and is_valid_multiword(category):
                ckey = normalize(category)
                if ckey not in seen:
                    seen.add(ckey)
                    entries.append(make_entry(category, "JOB_CATEGORY", 0.85))

    # Master file
    master = load_json(folder / "job_titles_master.json")
    if master and isinstance(master, dict):
        for title in master.get("job_titles", []):
            if isinstance(title, str):
                nkey = normalize(title)
                if nkey not in seen:
                    seen.add(nkey)
                    # Convert underscored filenames to proper titles
                    clean = title.replace("_", " ").strip()
                    if clean:
                        entries.append(make_entry(clean, "JOB_TITLE", 0.90))

    # Databases subfolder
    db_folder = folder / "databases"
    if db_folder.exists():
        for fp in db_folder.glob("*.json"):
            data = load_json(fp)
            if not data:
                continue
            if isinstance(data, dict):
                titles = data.get("normalized_job_titles", data.get("job_titles", []))
                if isinstance(titles, list):
                    for t in titles:
                        if isinstance(t, str):
                            nkey = normalize(t)
                            if nkey not in seen:
                                seen.add(nkey)
                                entries.append(make_entry(t, "JOB_TITLE", 0.90))

    if verbose:
        print(f"  job_titles/: {len(entries):,} job titles extracted")
    return entries


def mine_core_databases(verbose: bool = False) -> List[dict]:
    """Extract from core_databases/ — candidate DB (job titles), companies DB."""
    folder = AI_DATA / "core_databases"
    if not folder.exists():
        return []

    entries = []
    seen = set()

    # Candidate database — extract Job Title and Company fields
    cand_path = folder / "Candidate_database.json"
    if cand_path.exists():
        if verbose:
            print("    Loading Candidate_database.json (large file)...")
        try:
            with open(cand_path, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") as f:
                # Stream-parse to avoid loading 788K lines at once
                data = json.load(f)
            if isinstance(data, list):
                for record in data:
                    if not isinstance(record, dict):
                        continue
                    jt = record.get("Job Title", "").strip()
                    if jt and is_valid_multiword(jt):
                        nkey = normalize(jt)
                        if nkey not in seen:
                            seen.add(nkey)
                            entries.append(make_entry(jt, "JOB_TITLE", 0.80))
                if verbose:
                    print(f"    Candidate_database.json: {len(data):,} records → {len(entries):,} titles")
        except Exception as e:
            if verbose:
                print(f"    ERROR loading Candidate_database.json: {e}")

    # Companies database — extract company names (industry terms)
    comp_path = folder / "Companies_database.json"
    if comp_path.exists():
        if verbose:
            print("    Loading Companies_database.json...")
        comp_data = load_json(comp_path)
        company_count = 0
        if isinstance(comp_data, dict):
            for name in comp_data.keys():
                if is_valid_multiword(name) and len(name) > 5:
                    nkey = normalize(name)
                    if nkey not in seen:
                        seen.add(nkey)
                        # Classify the company description if it looks like an industry
                        cat = classify_term(name)
                        entries.append(make_entry(name, cat, 0.65))
                        company_count += 1
            if verbose:
                print(f"    Companies_database.json: {len(comp_data):,} entries → {company_count:,} industry terms")

    if verbose:
        print(f"  core_databases/: total {len(entries):,} terms")
    return entries


def mine_parsed_cvs(verbose: bool = False) -> List[dict]:
    """Extract from parsed_cv_files/ and root-level parsed document JSONs."""
    entries = []
    seen = set()

    # parsed_cv_files/cv_batch_0001.json
    batch_path = AI_DATA / "parsed_cv_files" / "cv_batch_0001.json"
    if batch_path.exists():
        data = load_json(batch_path)
        if isinstance(data, list):
            for cv in data:
                if isinstance(cv, dict):
                    text = cv.get("text", "")
                    extracted = extract_terms_from_text(text)
                    for phrase, label in extracted:
                        nkey = normalize(phrase)
                        if nkey not in seen:
                            seen.add(nkey)
                            entries.append(make_entry(phrase, label, 0.65))
            if verbose:
                print(f"  parsed_cv_files/: {len(data)} CVs → {len(entries):,} terms")

    # Root-level parsed DOCX JSONs (files with epoch timestamps)
    root_cv_count = 0
    for fp in AI_DATA.glob("*_17707*.json"):
        data = load_json(fp)
        if not data or not isinstance(data, dict):
            continue
        root_cv_count += 1
        extracted_data = data.get("extracted_data", {})
        text = extracted_data.get("text", "") if isinstance(extracted_data, dict) else ""
        if text:
            extracted = extract_terms_from_text(text)
            for phrase, label in extracted:
                nkey = normalize(phrase)
                if nkey not in seen:
                    seen.add(nkey)
                    entries.append(make_entry(phrase, label, 0.65))

    if verbose:
        print(f"  root parsed docs: {root_cv_count:,} files → {len(entries):,} total terms")
    return entries


def mine_root_databases(verbose: bool = False) -> List[dict]:
    """Extract from root-level database files."""
    entries = []
    seen = set()

    # enhanced_job_titles_database.json
    ejtd = load_json(AI_DATA / "enhanced_job_titles_database.json")
    if isinstance(ejtd, dict):
        for title in ejtd.get("normalized_job_titles", []):
            if isinstance(title, str):
                nkey = normalize(title)
                if nkey not in seen:
                    seen.add(nkey)
                    entries.append(make_entry(title, "JOB_TITLE", 0.90))

    # canonical_glossary.json
    cg = load_json(AI_DATA / "canonical_glossary.json")
    if isinstance(cg, dict):
        for abbrev, expansion in cg.items():
            if isinstance(expansion, str) and is_valid_multiword(expansion):
                nkey = normalize(expansion)
                if nkey not in seen:
                    seen.add(nkey)
                    entries.append(make_entry(expansion, "GLOSSARY_TERM", 0.85))

    # consolidated_terms.json — noisy, needs heavy filtering
    ct = load_json(AI_DATA / "consolidated_terms.json")
    if isinstance(ct, list):
        valid = 0
        for term in ct:
            if isinstance(term, str) and is_valid_multiword(term):
                nkey = normalize(term)
                if nkey not in seen and len(term) > 5:
                    seen.add(nkey)
                    label = classify_term(term)
                    entries.append(make_entry(term, label, 0.60))
                    valid += 1
        if verbose:
            print(f"  consolidated_terms.json: {len(ct):,} raw → {valid:,} valid terms")

    # companies_clean_all.json / companies_smart_validated_all.json
    for fname in ["companies_clean_all.json", "companies_smart_validated_all.json"]:
        fpath = AI_DATA / fname
        if fpath.exists():
            data = load_json(fpath)
            if isinstance(data, list):
                for item in data:
                    name = item if isinstance(item, str) else (
                        item.get("name", "") if isinstance(item, dict) else ""
                    )
                    if name and is_valid_multiword(name):
                        nkey = normalize(name)
                        if nkey not in seen:
                            seen.add(nkey)
                            entries.append(make_entry(name, classify_term(name), 0.65))
            elif isinstance(data, dict):
                for name in data.keys():
                    if is_valid_multiword(name) and len(name) > 5:
                        nkey = normalize(name)
                        if nkey not in seen:
                            seen.add(nkey)
                            entries.append(make_entry(name, classify_term(name), 0.65))

    # scraped_jobs.json
    sj = load_json(AI_DATA / "scraped_jobs.json")
    if isinstance(sj, list):
        for job in sj:
            if isinstance(job, dict):
                for field in ["title", "company", "skills", "description"]:
                    val = job.get(field, "")
                    if isinstance(val, str) and is_valid_multiword(val):
                        nkey = normalize(val)
                        if nkey not in seen:
                            seen.add(nkey)
                            label = "JOB_TITLE" if field == "title" else classify_term(val)
                            entries.append(make_entry(val, label, 0.75))
                    elif isinstance(val, list):
                        for item in val:
                            if isinstance(item, str) and is_valid_multiword(item):
                                nkey = normalize(item)
                                if nkey not in seen:
                                    seen.add(nkey)
                                    entries.append(make_entry(item, classify_term(item), 0.75))

    if verbose:
        print(f"  root databases: {len(entries):,} terms")
    return entries


def mine_text_glossaries(verbose: bool = False) -> List[dict]:
    """Extract from .txt glossary files in parent directory."""
    parent = AI_DATA.parent
    entries = []
    seen = set()

    txt_files = [
        "more data 2.txt",
        "more data for glossary.txt",
        "lists to add to the glossrys.txt",
    ]

    for fname in txt_files:
        fpath = parent / fname
        if not fpath.exists():
            continue
        try:
            with open(fpath, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") as f:
                content = f.read()
        except Exception:
            continue

        # Extract glossary terms: lines that look like definitions
        #   "TERM: definition" or "TERM - definition" or bullet items
        lines = content.split("\n")
        for line in lines:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line or len(line) < 5:
                continue

            # Skip pure prose / paragraph text
            if len(line) > 200:
                continue

            # Pattern: "TERM: definition" or "TERM - definition"
            match = re.match(r"^([A-Z][A-Za-z\s/&-]{2,50})[\s]*[-:–—]", line)
            if match:
                term = match.group(1).strip()
                if is_valid_multiword(term) or (is_valid_single_term(term) and len(term.split()) >= 2):
                    nkey = normalize(term)
                    if nkey not in seen:
                        seen.add(nkey)
                        entries.append(make_entry(term, classify_term(term), 0.80))
                continue

            # Pattern: bullet "• term" or "- term"
            match2 = re.match(r"^[\•\-\*]\s*(.{4,60})$", line)
            if match2:
                term = match2.group(1).strip()
                if is_valid_multiword(term):
                    nkey = normalize(term)
                    if nkey not in seen:
                        seen.add(nkey)
                        entries.append(make_entry(term, classify_term(term), 0.75))

    if verbose:
        print(f"  text glossaries: {len(entries):,} terms from {len(txt_files)} files")
    return entries


def mine_subfolder_jsons(folder_name: str, verbose: bool = False, max_files: int = 5000) -> List[dict]:
    """Generic miner for any subfolder containing JSON files with text data."""
    folder = AI_DATA / folder_name
    if not folder.exists() or not folder.is_dir():
        return []

    entries = []
    seen = set()
    count = 0

    if verbose:
        print(f"    scanning {folder_name}/...", end=" ", flush=True)

    for fp in folder.rglob("*.json"):
        if count >= max_files:
            if verbose:
                print(f"(capped at {max_files})", end=" ", flush=True)
            break
        data = load_json(fp)
        if not data:
            continue
        count += 1

        # Handle different JSON structures
        if isinstance(data, dict):
            # Extract from any text-like fields
            for key in ["text", "description", "title", "name", "skills",
                        "content", "summary", "body", "notes", "subject"]:
                val = data.get(key, "")
                if isinstance(val, str) and val:
                    for phrase, label in extract_terms_from_text(val):
                        nkey = normalize(phrase)
                        if nkey not in seen:
                            seen.add(nkey)
                            entries.append(make_entry(phrase, label, 0.65))
                elif isinstance(val, list):
                    for item in val:
                        if isinstance(item, str) and is_valid_multiword(item):
                            nkey = normalize(item)
                            if nkey not in seen:
                                seen.add(nkey)
                                entries.append(make_entry(item, classify_term(item), 0.65))

            # Recurse into extracted_data
            ed = data.get("extracted_data", {})
            if isinstance(ed, dict):
                text = ed.get("text", "")
                if text:
                    for phrase, label in extract_terms_from_text(text):
                        nkey = normalize(phrase)
                        if nkey not in seen:
                            seen.add(nkey)
                            entries.append(make_entry(phrase, label, 0.65))

        elif isinstance(data, list):
            for item in data:
                if isinstance(item, dict):
                    for key in ["text", "description", "title", "name", "skills"]:
                        val = item.get(key, "")
                        if isinstance(val, str) and is_valid_multiword(val):
                            nkey = normalize(val)
                            if nkey not in seen:
                                seen.add(nkey)
                                label = "JOB_TITLE" if key == "title" else classify_term(val)
                                entries.append(make_entry(val, label, 0.65))
                elif isinstance(item, str) and is_valid_multiword(item):
                    nkey = normalize(item)
                    if nkey not in seen:
                        seen.add(nkey)
                        entries.append(make_entry(item, classify_term(item), 0.65))

    if verbose and count > 0:
        print(f"  {folder_name}/: {count:,} files → {len(entries):,} terms")
    return entries


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#  NLP-LITE TERM EXTRACTION
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Known title patterns (regex-based)
TITLE_PATTERNS = re.compile(
    r"\b("
    r"(?:senior|junior|lead|principal|chief|head of|director of|manager of|"
    r"vp of|vice president of|assistant|associate|executive)\s+"
    r"[a-zA-Z\s&/-]{3,40}"
    r")\b",
    re.IGNORECASE,
)

# Skill patterns
SKILL_PATTERNS = re.compile(
    r"\b("
    r"(?:project management|data analysis|machine learning|"
    r"business intelligence|quality assurance|quality control|"
    r"supply chain|risk management|change management|"
    r"continuous improvement|business development|"
    r"stakeholder management|performance management|"
    r"talent acquisition|customer service|financial analysis|"
    r"process improvement|root cause analysis|lean manufacturing|"
    r"six sigma|regulatory compliance|technical support|"
    r"software development|systems engineering|"
    r"product development|account management|sales management|"
    r"health and safety|environmental management|"
    r"human resources|information technology|"
    r"digital transformation|strategic planning|cost reduction)"
    r")\b",
    re.IGNORECASE,
)

# Certification patterns
CERT_PATTERNS = re.compile(
    r"\b("
    r"(?:PMP|PRINCE2|NEBOSH|IOSH|CISSP|CISM|TOGAF|ITIL|"
    r"AWS Certified|Azure Certified|Google Certified|"
    r"Chartered Engineer|CEng|IEng|"
    r"Six Sigma (?:Green|Black|Yellow) Belt|"
    r"ISO \d{4,5}|OHSAS \d+|"
    r"CCNA|CCNP|CCIE|CompTIA|"
    r"CPA|CFA|ACCA|CIMA|ACA)"
    r")\b",
    re.IGNORECASE,
)

def extract_terms_from_text(text: str) -> List[Tuple[str, str]]:
    """
    Extract multi-word terms from free text using pattern matching.
    Returns list of (phrase, label) tuples.
    """
    if not text or len(text) < 20:
        return []

    results = []
    seen_in_text = set()

    # 1. Title patterns
    for m in TITLE_PATTERNS.finditer(text):
        phrase = m.group(1).strip()
        if is_valid_multiword(phrase):
            nkey = normalize(phrase)
            if nkey not in seen_in_text:
                seen_in_text.add(nkey)
                results.append((phrase, "JOB_TITLE"))

    # 2. Skill patterns
    for m in SKILL_PATTERNS.finditer(text):
        phrase = m.group(1).strip()
        nkey = normalize(phrase)
        if nkey not in seen_in_text:
            seen_in_text.add(nkey)
            cat = classify_term(phrase)
            results.append((phrase, cat))

    # 3. Certification patterns
    for m in CERT_PATTERNS.finditer(text):
        phrase = m.group(1).strip()
        nkey = normalize(phrase)
        if nkey not in seen_in_text:
            seen_in_text.add(nkey)
            results.append((phrase, "CERTIFICATION"))

    # 4. N-gram extraction for capitalized multi-word phrases
    #    (heuristic: consecutive Title Case words = likely a named concept)
    words = text.split()
    i = 0
    while i < len(words) - 1:
        # Find runs of capitalized words
        if words[i] and words[i][0].isupper() and len(words[i]) > 1:
            phrase_words = [words[i]]
            j = i + 1
            while j < len(words) and j - i < 6:
                w = words[j]
                # Allow lowercase connectors (of, and, for, in) within the phrase
                if w.lower() in ("of", "and", "for", "in", "&", "/"):
                    if j + 1 < len(words) and words[j + 1] and words[j + 1][0].isupper():
                        phrase_words.append(w)
                        j += 1
                        continue
                    else:
                        break
                elif w and w[0].isupper() and len(w) > 1:
                    phrase_words.append(w)
                    j += 1
                else:
                    break
            if len(phrase_words) >= 2:
                phrase = " ".join(phrase_words)
                if is_valid_multiword(phrase):
                    nkey = normalize(phrase)
                    if nkey not in seen_in_text:
                        seen_in_text.add(nkey)
                        cat = classify_term(phrase)
                        results.append((phrase, cat))
            i = j
        else:
            i += 1

    return results


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#  MERGE LOGIC
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Map labels to output files
LABEL_TO_FILE = {
    "TECH_SKILL": "tech_skills.json",
    "ESCO_SKILL": "tech_skills.json",
    "KNOWLEDGE": "tech_skills.json",
    "CLOUD_DEVOPS": "cloud_devops.json",
    "SOFT_SKILL": "soft_skills.json",
    "JOB_TITLE": "job_titles.json",
    "JOB_CATEGORY": "job_titles.json",
    "CERTIFICATION": "certifications.json",
    "METHODOLOGY": "certifications.json",
    "INDUSTRIAL_SKILL": "industrial_terms.json",
    "ENGINEERING": "engineering.json",
    "OIL_GAS": "oil_gas.json",
    "FINANCIAL_SERVICES": "financial_services.json",
    "BIOTECH_PHARMA": "biotech_pharma.json",
    "MANUFACTURING": "manufacturing.json",
    "MARKETING": "marketing.json",
    "HR_RECRUITMENT": "hr_recruitment.json",
    "GLOSSARY": "industrial_terms.json",
    "GLOSSARY_TERM": "industrial_terms.json",
    "CV_SKILL": "cv_mined_phrases.json",
    "CV_MINED": "cv_mined_phrases.json",
    "DB_MODEL": "tech_skills.json",
    "DB_COLUMN": "tech_skills.json",
    "API_RESOURCE": "tech_skills.json",
    "UI_PAGE": "tech_skills.json",
}

def merge_into_files(all_entries: List[dict], apply: bool, verbose: bool) -> Dict[str, Tuple[int, int]]:
    """
    Merge all extracted entries into the appropriate collocation JSON files.
    Returns dict of filename → (existing_count, new_count).
    """
    # Group entries by target file
    grouped = defaultdict(list)
    for entry in all_entries:
        label = entry.get("label", "INDUSTRIAL_SKILL")
        target = LABEL_TO_FILE.get(label, "industrial_terms.json")
        grouped[target].append(entry)

    results = {}
    total_new = 0

    for filename, entries in sorted(grouped.items()):
        filepath = COLLOCATION_DIR / filename
        existing = load_existing_phrases(filepath)
        existing_count = len(existing)

        # Filter out duplicates
        new_entries = []
        for entry in entries:
            nkey = normalize(entry["phrase"])
            if nkey not in existing and len(nkey) > 2:
                existing.add(nkey)  # prevent intra-batch dupes
                new_entries.append(entry)

        results[filename] = (existing_count, len(new_entries))
        total_new += len(new_entries)

        if apply and new_entries:
            # Load existing file data
            existing_data = load_json(filepath) if filepath.exists() else []
            if not isinstance(existing_data, list):
                existing_data = []
            existing_data.extend(new_entries)

            # Write back
            filepath.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            with open(filepath, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                json.dump(existing_data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)

        if verbose:
            status = "WRITTEN" if apply else "DRY-RUN"
            print(f"  {filename:40s} existing={existing_count:>6,}  new={len(new_entries):>6,}  [{status}]")

    return results


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#  MANIFEST UPDATE
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def update_manifest(results: Dict[str, Tuple[int, int]], apply: bool):
    """Update the collocation manifest with current stats."""
    manifest_path = COLLOCATION_DIR / "_manifest.json"
    manifest = load_json(manifest_path) or {}

    if not isinstance(manifest, dict):
        manifest = {}

    manifest["last_full_ingestion"] = TODAY
    manifest["version"] = manifest.get("version", "1.0.0")
    manifest["ingestion_source"] = SOURCE_TAG

    files_section = manifest.get("files", {})
    for filename, (existing, new) in results.items():
        files_section[filename] = {
            "total_phrases": existing + new,
            "new_from_ingestion": new,
            "last_updated": TODAY,
        }
    manifest["files"] = files_section

    if apply:
        with open(manifest_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            json.dump(manifest, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#  MAIN
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def main():
    import argparse
    import io
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="CareerTrojan Full AI Data Ingestion")
    parser.add_argument("--apply", action="store_true", help="Write changes (default: dry-run)")
    parser.add_argument("--verbose", action="store_true", help="Show detailed progress")
    parser.add_argument("--stats-only", action="store_true", help="Show data statistics only")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    # Tee output to log file
    log_path = PROJECT_ROOT / "logs" / "ingestion_log.txt"
    log_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    class Tee:
        def __init__(self, *streams):
            self.streams = streams
        def write(self, data):
            for s in self.streams:
                s.write(data)
                s.flush()
        def flush(self):
            for s in self.streams:
                s.flush()

    log_file = open(log_path, "w", encoding="utf-8")
    # Use the already-reconfigured stdout, not the raw __stdout__
    _real_stdout = sys.stdout
    sys.stdout = Tee(_real_stdout, log_file)
    print(f"[Log file: {log_path}]")

    print("\n" + "=" * 60)
    print("  CareerTrojan — Full AI Data Ingestion Pipeline")
    print("=" * 60)
    print(f"  AI Data:        {AI_DATA}")
    print(f"  Collocation:    {COLLOCATION_DIR}")
    print(f"  Mode:           {'APPLY' if args.apply else 'DRY-RUN'}")
    print(f"  Date:           {TODAY}")
    print("=" * 60 + "\n")

    if not AI_DATA.exists():
        print(f"ERROR: AI data directory not found: {AI_DATA}")
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.stats_only:
        show_stats()
        return

    stats = IngestionStats()
    all_entries = []

    # ── Phase 1: Structured data sources ──────────────────────
    print("Phase 1: Mining structured data sources...")

    print("  [1/8] Job titles folder...")
    jt = mine_job_titles_folder(args.verbose)
    all_entries.extend(jt)
    stats.terms_extracted["job_titles_folder"] = len(jt)

    print("  [2/8] Core databases...")
    cd = mine_core_databases(args.verbose)
    all_entries.extend(cd)
    stats.terms_extracted["core_databases"] = len(cd)

    print("  [3/8] Root-level databases...")
    rd = mine_root_databases(args.verbose)
    all_entries.extend(rd)
    stats.terms_extracted["root_databases"] = len(rd)

    print("  [4/8] Contacts folder...")
    ct = mine_contacts(args.verbose)
    all_entries.extend(ct)
    stats.terms_extracted["contacts"] = len(ct)

    # ── Phase 2: Text mining ──────────────────────────────────
    print("\nPhase 2: Mining text content...")

    print("  [5/8] Profiles (career summaries)...")
    pf = mine_profiles(args.verbose)
    all_entries.extend(pf)
    stats.terms_extracted["profiles"] = len(pf)

    print("  [6/8] Parsed CVs + root documents...")
    pc = mine_parsed_cvs(args.verbose)
    all_entries.extend(pc)
    stats.terms_extracted["parsed_cvs"] = len(pc)

    print("  [7/8] Text glossaries...")
    tg = mine_text_glossaries(args.verbose)
    all_entries.extend(tg)
    stats.terms_extracted["text_glossaries"] = len(tg)

    # ── Phase 3: Remaining subfolders ─────────────────────────
    print("\nPhase 3: Mining remaining subfolders...")

    SUBFOLDERS_TO_MINE = [
        "emails", "email_extracted",
        "enrichment_results", "data_cloud_solutions",
        "exported_cvs", "exports",
        "job_matches", "job_matching",
        "job descriptions",  # space variant
        "journal_entries",
        "learning_library",
        "locations",
        "metadata", "normalized", "notes",
        "parsed_from_automated", "parsed_job_descriptions", "parsed_resumes",
        "processed_cvs",
        "demo_data",
        "cv_files",
    ]

    SKIP_FOLDERS = {
        "graphics_templates", "heatmaps", "visualizations",
        "logs", "duplicates", "Archive",
        "collocation_data",  # already processed separately
        "core_databases",    # already processed
        "contacts",          # already processed
        "profiles",          # already processed
        "job_titles",        # already processed
        "parsed_cv_files",   # already processed
        "Industries",        # empty
        "job_descriptions",  # empty
        "USER DATA",         # raw uploads
        "user_uploads",      # raw uploads
        "automated_parser",  # 155K+ raw files, too large
        "__pycache__",
        ".git",
        "node_modules",
    }

    count_8 = 0
    for subfolder in SUBFOLDERS_TO_MINE:
        if args.verbose:
            print(f"    checking {subfolder}/...", flush=True)
        sf_entries = mine_subfolder_jsons(subfolder, args.verbose)
        if sf_entries:
            all_entries.extend(sf_entries)
            stats.terms_extracted[f"subfolder_{subfolder}"] = len(sf_entries)
            count_8 += len(sf_entries)

    # Also check any folders we haven't explicitly handled
    if AI_DATA.exists():
        handled = set(SUBFOLDERS_TO_MINE) | SKIP_FOLDERS | {sf.replace(" ", "_") for sf in SUBFOLDERS_TO_MINE}
        for item in AI_DATA.iterdir():
            if item.is_dir() and item.name not in handled:
                if args.verbose:
                    print(f"    auto-scanning {item.name}/...", flush=True)
                sf_entries = mine_subfolder_jsons(item.name, args.verbose, max_files=2000)
                if sf_entries:
                    all_entries.extend(sf_entries)
                    stats.terms_extracted[f"subfolder_{item.name}"] = len(sf_entries)
                    count_8 += len(sf_entries)

    print(f"  [8/8] Subfolders: {count_8:,} additional terms\n")

    # ── Deduplicate across all sources ────────────────────────
    print("Deduplicating across all sources...")
    seen_global = set()
    unique_entries = []
    for entry in all_entries:
        nkey = normalize(entry["phrase"])
        if nkey not in seen_global:
            seen_global.add(nkey)
            unique_entries.append(entry)

    print(f"  Raw terms:    {len(all_entries):,}")
    print(f"  After dedup:  {len(unique_entries):,}")
    print(f"  Duplicates:   {len(all_entries) - len(unique_entries):,}\n")

    # ── Merge into collocation files ─────────────────────────
    print("Merging into collocation files...")
    results = merge_into_files(unique_entries, args.apply, args.verbose)

    # ── Update manifest ──────────────────────────────────────
    update_manifest(results, args.apply)

    # ── Final report ─────────────────────────────────────────
    print("\n" + "=" * 60)
    print("  INGESTION COMPLETE")
    print("=" * 60)

    total_existing = sum(r[0] for r in results.values())
    total_new = sum(r[1] for r in results.values())

    print(f"\n  Pre-existing terms:  {total_existing:,}")
    print(f"  New terms added:     {total_new:,}")
    print(f"  Total terms now:     {total_existing + total_new:,}")
    print(f"\n  Files updated:")
    for filename, (existing, new) in sorted(results.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1][1]):
        marker = "✓" if new > 0 else " "
        print(f"    {marker} {filename:40s} {existing + new:>8,}  (+{new:,})")

    if not args.apply:
        print(f"\n  ⚠  DRY RUN — no files changed. Use --apply to write.")
    else:
        print(f"\n  ✓  All changes written to {COLLOCATION_DIR}")

    print("=" * 60 + "\n")

    # Term category breakdown
    category_counts = Counter()
    for entry in unique_entries:
        category_counts[entry["label"]] += 1
    print("  Term category breakdown:")
    for cat, count in category_counts.most_common(20):
        print(f"    {cat:30s} {count:>8,}")
    print()

    # Close log
    sys.stdout = sys.__stdout__
    log_file.close()
    print(f"\nFull log saved to: {log_path}")


def show_stats():
    """Show data statistics without mining."""
    print("\n  AI Data Statistics:")
    print("  " + "-" * 50)

    folders_to_count = [
        "contacts", "profiles", "job_titles", "parsed_cv_files",
        "core_databases", "collocation_data", "emails", "email_extracted",
        "enrichment_results", "exported_cvs", "job_matches",
        "parsed_resumes", "parsed_job_descriptions", "locations",
    ]

    # Count root JSON files
    root_jsons = list(AI_DATA.glob("*.json"))
    print(f"  Root JSON files:     {len(root_jsons):>8,}")

    for folder in folders_to_count:
        fpath = AI_DATA / folder
        if fpath.exists() and fpath.is_dir():
            count = sum(1 for _ in fpath.rglob("*.json"))
            print(f"  {folder + '/':25s} {count:>8,} JSON files")
        else:
            print(f"  {folder + '/':25s}      (not found)")

    # Existing collocation terms
    print(f"\n  Existing collocation terms:")
    if COLLOCATION_DIR.exists():
        for fp in sorted(COLLOCATION_DIR.glob("*.json")):
            if fp.name == "_manifest.json":
                continue
            data = load_json(fp)
            count = len(data) if isinstance(data, list) else 0
            print(f"    {fp.name:40s} {count:>8,}")
    print()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
