"""
test_auth_routes.py — Integration tests for the auth flow.

Tests register → login → refresh → verify → password-reset → logout.
"""
import pytest


class TestAuthRegisterLogin:
    """Registration and login flow."""

    def test_register_returns_user(self, client):
        payload = {
            "email": "newuser@test.com",
            "password": "StrongPass123!",
            "full_name": "New User",
        }
        r = client.post("/api/v1/auth/register", json=payload)
        assert r.status_code in (200, 201, 422), f"Register: {r.status_code} {r.text}"

    def test_login_returns_token(self, client):
        payload = {"username": "testuser@careertrojan.com", "password": "TestPass123!"}
        r = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", data=payload)
        # Could be 200 (success) or 401 (no real user in test DB)
        assert r.status_code in (200, 401, 422)

    def test_login_invalid_credentials_401(self, client):
        payload = {"username": "nobody@fake.com", "password": "wrong"}
        r = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", data=payload)
        assert r.status_code in (401, 422)

    def test_refresh_without_token_fails(self, client_no_auth):
        r = client_no_auth.post("/api/v1/auth/refresh")
        assert r.status_code in (401, 422)

    def test_password_reset_request(self, client):
        r = client.post("/api/v1/auth/password-reset/request", json={"email": "test@test.com"})
        # Should accept gracefully even if user doesn't exist
        assert r.status_code in (200, 404, 422)

    def test_logout(self, client):
        r = client.post("/api/v1/auth/logout")
        assert r.status_code in (200, 204)


class TestAuthProtection:
    """Verify endpoints return 401 without auth."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("method,path", [
        ("GET", "/api/v1/user/profile"),
        ("GET", "/api/v1/user/activity"),
        ("GET", "/api/v1/coaching/sessions"),
        ("GET", "/api/v1/credits/balance"),
        ("GET", "/api/v1/matching/results"),
        ("GET", "/api/v1/payment/plans"),
        ("POST", "/api/v1/resume/upload"),
    ])
    def test_protected_endpoints_require_auth(self, client_no_auth, method, path):
        if method == "GET":
            r = client_no_auth.get(path)
        else:
            r = client_no_auth.post(path)
        assert r.status_code in (401, 403), f"{method} {path} returned {r.status_code}"


Is this the best Collocation Engine - If not look at BEST of BREED

"""
Collocation Engine — CareerTrojan
====================================
Multi-word expression detection using statistical + rule-based methods.

The core problem: standard tokenizers see "Machine Learning" as two separate
tokens, losing the semantic bond. This engine detects and preserves these
multi-word units using five complementary techniques:

  1. N-gram Extraction        — bigrams/trigrams as first-class tokens
  2. PMI Scoring               — statistical "glue" score for word pairs
  3. spaCy PhraseMatcher       — exact match on known domain phrases
  4. spaCy EntityRuler         — pattern-based custom NER (SKILL, CERT, etc.)
  5. Window Co-occurrence      — sliding window for near-but-not-adjacent pairs

Plus two knowledge-graph features:

  6. Term Evolution Chains     — links MRP I→MRP II→ERP as the same domain
                                  evolving over time, surfacing career
                                  progression paths for users.
  7. Skill Synonyms            — concurrent alternative names, regional
                                  spellings, abbreviations ("NLP" ↔
                                  "natural language processing").

Usage:
    from backend.app.services.collocation_engine import collocation_engine

    # Statistical collocation discovery
    collocations = collocation_engine.find_collocations(corpus_texts)

    # Frozen-phrase tokenization (preserves "machine learning" as one token)
    tokens = collocation_engine.tokenize_with_phrases("Senior machine learning engineer")
    # → ["senior", "machine_learning", "engineer"]

    # PhraseMatcher for known terms
    matches = collocation_engine.match_domain_phrases("Must know Ladder Logic and PLC")
    # → [{"phrase": "Ladder Logic", "label": "INDUSTRIAL_SKILL", "start": 10, "end": 22}]

    # Custom NER via EntityRuler
    entities = collocation_engine.extract_domain_entities(text)
    # → {"TECH_SKILL": ["React.js", "Node.js"], "CERT": ["AWS Solutions Architect"]}

    # N-gram extraction
    bigrams = collocation_engine.extract_ngrams("blue hydrogen economy", n=2)
    # → [("blue", "hydrogen"), ("hydrogen", "economy")]

    # Window co-occurrence
    pairs = collocation_engine.window_cooccurrence(texts, window=5, min_freq=3)

    # Evolution chain resolution
    chain = collocation_engine.resolve_evolution("MRP II")
    # → {"chain_id": "erp_evolution", "current_term": "enterprise resource planning",
    #     "career_insight": "If you worked with MRP systems...", ...}

    # Synonym resolution
    canonical = collocation_engine.resolve_synonym("NLP")
    # → "natural language processing"

Author: CareerTrojan System
Date: February 2026
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import logging
import math
import os
import re
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Set, Tuple

logger = logging.getLogger("careertrojan.collocation_engine")


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DATA DIRECTORY — collocation JSON files loaded at runtime
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Resolution order:
#   1. COLLOCATION_DATA_DIR environment variable
#   2. AI_DATA_PATH/collocation_data (from runtime.env)
#   3. Known dev paths (L: drive, local data/)
#   4. Empty dict fallback (engine still works, just no phrases)

def _resolve_data_dir() -> Optional[Path]:
    """Find the collocation_data directory."""
    # 1. Explicit env var
    explicit = os.environ.get("COLLOCATION_DATA_DIR")
    if explicit:
        p = Path(explicit)
        if p.is_dir():
            return p

    # 2. AI_DATA_PATH from runtime.env
    ai_data = os.environ.get("AI_DATA_PATH")
    if ai_data:
        p = Path(ai_data) / "collocation_data"
        if p.is_dir():
            return p

    # 3. Known dev paths
    candidates = [
        Path(r"L:\VS ai_data final - version\ai_data_final\collocation_data"),
        Path(__file__).resolve().parents[4] / "data" / "ai_data_final" / "collocation_data",
        Path.cwd() / "data" / "ai_data_final" / "collocation_data",
    ]
    for c in candidates:
        if c.is_dir():
            return c

    return None


def _load_phrase_file(filepath: Path) -> Dict[str, str]:
    """
    Load a single collocation JSON file → {phrase: label} dict.

    Expected format (list of entries):
        [{"phrase": "machine learning", "label": "TECH_SKILL", ...}, ...]
    """
    try:
        with open(filepath, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            data = json.load(f)
        if not isinstance(data, list):
            logger.warning("Skipping %s: expected list, got %s", filepath.name, type(data).__name__)
            return {}
        phrases = {}
        for entry in data:
            if isinstance(entry, dict) and "phrase" in entry and "label" in entry:
                phrase = entry["phrase"].strip().lower()
                label = entry["label"].strip()
                if phrase and label:
                    phrases[phrase] = label
        return phrases
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("Failed to load %s: %s", filepath, e)
        return {}


def _load_abbreviation_file(filepath: Path) -> Dict[str, str]:
    """
    Load abbreviations.json → {abbr: expansion} dict.

    Expected format (flat dict):
        {"plc": "programmable logic controller", ...}
    """
    try:
        with open(filepath, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            data = json.load(f)
        if not isinstance(data, dict):
            logger.warning("Skipping %s: expected dict, got %s", filepath.name, type(data).__name__)
            return {}
        return {k.strip().lower(): v.strip().lower() for k, v in data.items() if k and v}
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("Failed to load %s: %s", filepath, e)
        return {}


def _load_all_from_directory(data_dir: Optional[Path]) -> Tuple[Dict[str, str], Dict[str, str], Dict[str, int]]:
    """
    Load all collocation data from a directory of JSON files.

    Returns:
        (phrases_dict, abbreviations_dict, source_stats)
    """
    phrases: Dict[str, str] = {}
    abbreviations: Dict[str, str] = {}
    stats: Dict[str, int] = {}

    if data_dir is None or not data_dir.is_dir():
        logger.warning("CollocationEngine: no data directory found — running with empty gazetteer")
        return phrases, abbreviations, stats

    for json_file in sorted(data_dir.glob("*.json")):
        if json_file.name.startswith("_"):
            continue  # skip _manifest.json etc.

        if json_file.name == "abbreviations.json":
            abbreviations = _load_abbreviation_file(json_file)
            stats["abbreviations"] = len(abbreviations)
        else:
            file_phrases = _load_phrase_file(json_file)
            stats[json_file.stem] = len(file_phrases)
            phrases.update(file_phrases)

    logger.info(
        "CollocationEngine: loaded %d phrases + %d abbreviations from %s (%d files)",
        len(phrases), len(abbreviations), data_dir, len(stats),
    )
    return phrases, abbreviations, stats


def _load_evolution_chains(data_dir: Optional[Path]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    """
    Load term_evolution.json — nomenclature evolution chains.

    Each chain links legacy terms to their modern equivalents so the AI
    recognises that MRP I → MRP II → ERP are the SAME domain evolving.
    """
    if data_dir is None:
        return []
    fp = data_dir / "term_evolution.json"
    if not fp.exists():
        return []
    try:
        with open(fp, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            data = json.load(f)
        chains = data.get("chains", [])
        logger.info("CollocationEngine: loaded %d evolution chains", len(chains))
        return chains
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("Failed to load term_evolution.json: %s", e)
        return []


def _build_evolution_index(
    chains: List[Dict[str, Any]],
) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:
    """
    Build a lookup index: normalised term → chain + position info.

    Any term (legacy or current) or abbreviation appearing in any chain
    is indexed so a single lookup resolves the full evolutionary context.
    """
    index: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
    for chain in chains:
        chain_id = chain.get("chain_id", "")
        for step in chain.get("evolution", []):
            term = step.get("term", "").strip().lower()
            abbrev = (step.get("abbrev") or "").strip().lower()
            entry = {
                "chain_id": chain_id,
                "chain": chain,
                "matched_step": step,
            }
            if term:
                index[term] = entry
            if abbrev:
                index[abbrev] = entry
        # Also index the current_term and current_abbrev at chain level
        ct = (chain.get("current_term") or "").strip().lower()
        ca = (chain.get("current_abbrev") or "").strip().lower()
        if ct and ct not in index:
            index[ct] = {"chain_id": chain_id, "chain": chain, "matched_step": None}
        if ca and ca not in index:
            index[ca] = {"chain_id": chain_id, "chain": chain, "matched_step": None}
    return index


def _load_synonyms(data_dir: Optional[Path]) -> Dict[str, List[str]]:
    """
    Load skill_synonyms.json — concurrent alternative names.

    Returns:
        {canonical_term: [alt1, alt2, ...]}
    """
    if data_dir is None:
        return {}
    fp = data_dir / "skill_synonyms.json"
    if not fp.exists():
        return {}
    try:
        with open(fp, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            data = json.load(f)
        synonyms = data.get("synonyms", {})
        logger.info("CollocationEngine: loaded %d synonym entries", len(synonyms))
        return synonyms
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("Failed to load skill_synonyms.json: %s", e)
        return {}


def _build_synonym_index(synonyms: Dict[str, List[str]]) -> Dict[str, str]:
    """
    Build a reverse lookup: any variant → canonical term.

    "nlp" → "natural language processing"
    "NLP" → "natural language processing"
    "natural language processing" → "natural language processing" (self-maps)
    """
    index: Dict[str, str] = {}
    for canonical, variants in synonyms.items():
        c_lower = canonical.strip().lower()
        index[c_lower] = c_lower  # self-map
        for v in variants:
            v_lower = v.strip().lower()
            if v_lower:
                index[v_lower] = c_lower
    return index


def _load_user_learned(data_dir: Optional[Path]) -> Dict[str, str]:
    """
    Load user_learned_terms.json — terms auto-discovered from user CV parsing.

    Format: [{"phrase": "...", "label": "...", "source": "user_cv", ...}]
    Returns {phrase: label} dict (same as _load_phrase_file).
    """
    if data_dir is None:
        return {}
    fp = data_dir / "user_learned_terms.json"
    if not fp.exists():
        return {}
    return _load_phrase_file(fp)


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# COLLOCATION ENGINE
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class CollocationEngine:
    """
    Multi-word expression detection and phrase-aware tokenization.

    Combines five methods:
      1. Domain gazetteer (exact phrase matching via spaCy PhraseMatcher)
      2. Custom NER (spaCy EntityRuler with pattern rules)
      3. Statistical collocation (PMI/chi-squared via NLTK)
      4. N-gram extraction (bigram/trigram generation)
      5. Window co-occurrence (sliding window word-pair frequency)

    The engine integrates with NLPGateway: it uses the same spaCy models
    but adds PhraseMatcher + EntityRuler on top.
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self._phrase_matcher = None       # spaCy PhraseMatcher
        self._entity_ruler = None         # spaCy EntityRuler
        self._nlp_for_matcher = None      # spaCy model powering matchers
        self._learned_collocations: Dict[Tuple[str, str], float] = {}  # (w1,w2)->pmi
        self._nltk_loaded = False

        # Load phrases + abbreviations from JSON files
        self._data_dir = _resolve_data_dir()
        phrases, abbreviations, source_stats = _load_all_from_directory(self._data_dir)
        self._custom_phrases: Dict[str, str] = phrases
        self._abbreviation_map: Dict[str, str] = abbreviations
        self._source_stats: Dict[str, int] = source_stats

        # Load evolution chains (MRP I→MRP II→ERP, Basel I→III, etc.)
        self._evolution_chains = _load_evolution_chains(self._data_dir)
        self._evolution_index = _build_evolution_index(self._evolution_chains)

        # Load skill synonyms (NLP ↔ "natural language processing")
        self._synonyms_raw = _load_synonyms(self._data_dir)
        self._synonym_index = _build_synonym_index(self._synonyms_raw)

        # Load user-learned terms (auto-populated from CV parsing)
        user_terms = _load_user_learned(self._data_dir)
        if user_terms:
            self._custom_phrases.update(user_terms)
            self._source_stats["user_learned_terms"] = len(user_terms)

    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # 1. DOMAIN PHRASE MATCHER (spaCy PhraseMatcher)
    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def _ensure_matcher(self) -> None:
        """Lazily build PhraseMatcher + EntityRuler from the gazetteer."""
        if self._phrase_matcher is not None:
            return

        try:
            import spacy
            from spacy.matcher import PhraseMatcher
        except ImportError:
            raise RuntimeError("spaCy required: pip install spacy")

        # Load small model for matching (fast, low memory)
        try:
            nlp = spacy.load("en_core_web_sm")
        except OSError:
            raise RuntimeError(
                "spaCy model not found. Run: python -m spacy download en_core_web_sm"
            )

        self._nlp_for_matcher = nlp

        # Build PhraseMatcher
        matcher = PhraseMatcher(nlp.vocab, attr="LOWER")  # case-insensitive

        # Group phrases by label so PhraseMatcher can identify them
        label_phrases: Dict[str, list] = defaultdict(list)
        for phrase, label in self._custom_phrases.items():
            doc = nlp.make_doc(phrase)
            label_phrases[label].append(doc)

        for label, docs in label_phrases.items():
            matcher.add(label, docs)

        self._phrase_matcher = matcher

        # Build EntityRuler with pattern-based rules
        self._build_entity_ruler(nlp)

        logger.info(
            "CollocationEngine: PhraseMatcher loaded with %d phrases, "
            "EntityRuler loaded with %d patterns",
            len(self._custom_phrases),
            len(self._entity_ruler.patterns) if self._entity_ruler else 0,
        )

    def _build_entity_ruler(self, nlp) -> None:
        """Build spaCy EntityRuler for pattern-based NER."""
        try:
            from spacy.language import Language

            # Create EntityRuler (or SpanRuler in spaCy 3.6+)
            if "entity_ruler" not in nlp.pipe_names:
                ruler = nlp.add_pipe("entity_ruler", before="ner")
            else:
                ruler = nlp.get_pipe("entity_ruler")

            patterns = []

            # Pattern: <N>+ years of experience
            patterns.append({
                "label": "EXPERIENCE_REQ",
                "pattern": [
                    {"LIKE_NUM": True},
                    {"LOWER": {"IN": ["+", "plus"]}, "OP": "?"},
                    {"LOWER": {"IN": ["year", "years"]}},
                    {"LOWER": {"IN": ["of", "in"]}, "OP": "?"},
                    {"LOWER": "experience", "OP": "?"},
                ],
            })

            # Pattern: Bachelor's/Master's/PhD degree
            patterns.append({
                "label": "EDUCATION_REQ",
                "pattern": [
                    {"LOWER": {"IN": [
                        "bachelor", "bachelors", "bachelor's",
                        "master", "masters", "master's",
                        "phd", "doctorate", "mba",
                    ]}},
                    {"LOWER": {"IN": ["degree", "s"]}, "OP": "?"},
                ],
            })

            # Pattern: BS/MS/PhD in <field>
            patterns.append({
                "label": "EDUCATION_REQ",
                "pattern": [
                    {"LOWER": {"IN": ["bs", "ms", "phd", "mba", "ba", "ma"]}},
                    {"LOWER": "in"},
                    {"POS": {"IN": ["NOUN", "PROPN"]}, "OP": "+"},
                ],
            })

            # Pattern: proficient/expert in <something>
            patterns.append({
                "label": "SKILL_REQ",
                "pattern": [
                    {"LOWER": {"IN": [
                        "proficient", "expert", "experienced",
                        "strong", "skilled", "fluent",
                    ]}},
                    {"LOWER": {"IN": ["in", "with"]}},
                    {"POS": {"IN": ["NOUN", "PROPN", "ADJ"]}, "OP": "+"},
                ],
            })

            # Add all gazetteer phrases as entity patterns too
            for phrase_text, label in self._custom_phrases.items():
                words = phrase_text.split()
                pattern = [{"LOWER": w.lower()} for w in words]
                patterns.append({"label": label, "pattern": pattern})

            ruler.add_patterns(patterns)
            self._entity_ruler = ruler

        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning("EntityRuler creation failed (continuing without): %s", e)
            self._entity_ruler = None

    def match_domain_phrases(
        self,
        text: str,
        *,
        max_chars: int = 100_000,
    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Find known domain phrases in text using PhraseMatcher.

        Returns list of matches:
            [{"phrase": "machine learning", "label": "TECH_SKILL",
              "start_char": 14, "end_char": 30}]
        """
        self._ensure_matcher()
        nlp = self._nlp_for_matcher
        doc = nlp.make_doc(text[:max_chars])

        # Run only tokenizer (no NER/parser needed for PhraseMatcher)
        matches = self._phrase_matcher(doc)

        results = []
        seen = set()
        for match_id, start, end in matches:
            span = doc[start:end]
            key = (span.text.lower(), start)
            if key in seen:
                continue
            seen.add(key)
            label = nlp.vocab.strings[match_id]
            results.append({
                "phrase": span.text,
                "label": label,
                "start_char": span[0].idx,
                "end_char": span[-1].idx + len(span[-1].text),
            })

        return results

    def extract_domain_entities(
        self,
        text: str,
        *,
        max_chars: int = 100_000,
    ) -> Dict[str, List[str]]:
        """
        Run full NLP pipeline with EntityRuler for custom domain NER.

        Returns dict of label → unique surface forms:
            {"TECH_SKILL": ["React.js", "Machine Learning"],
             "CERTIFICATION": ["AWS Solutions Architect"],
             "EXPERIENCE_REQ": ["5+ years of experience"]}
        """
        self._ensure_matcher()
        nlp = self._nlp_for_matcher
        doc = nlp(text[:max_chars])

        entities: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}
        for ent in doc.ents:
            entities.setdefault(ent.label_, [])
            normalised = ent.text.strip()
            if normalised not in entities[ent.label_]:
                entities[ent.label_].append(normalised)

        return entities

    def add_phrases(self, phrases: Dict[str, str]) -> None:
        """
        Add new phrases to the gazetteer at runtime.

        Args:
            phrases: {"blue hydrogen": "INDUSTRY_TERM", "ladder logic": "INDUSTRIAL_SKILL"}
        """
        self._custom_phrases.update(phrases)
        # Force matcher rebuild on next use
        self._phrase_matcher = None
        self._entity_ruler = None
        self._nlp_for_matcher = None
        logger.info("CollocationEngine: added %d phrases, matcher will rebuild", len(phrases))

    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # 1b. TERM EVOLUTION CHAINS
    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def resolve_evolution(self, term: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Resolve a term to its full evolution chain.

        If 'MRP II' is passed, returns the chain showing MRP→MRP II→ERP
        with career insights and the current modern equivalent.

        Args:
            term: any term or abbreviation (case-insensitive)

        Returns:
            dict with chain_id, current_term, evolution steps, career_insight,
            related_terms — or None if no chain found.
        """
        key = term.strip().lower()
        hit = self._evolution_index.get(key)
        if not hit:
            return None

        chain = hit["chain"]
        matched_step = hit.get("matched_step")
        return {
            "chain_id": chain.get("chain_id"),
            "domain": chain.get("domain"),
            "current_term": chain.get("current_term"),
            "current_abbrev": chain.get("current_abbrev"),
            "evolution": chain.get("evolution", []),
            "related_terms": chain.get("related_terms", []),
            "career_insight": chain.get("career_insight", ""),
            "matched_term": matched_step.get("term") if matched_step else key,
            "matched_status": matched_step.get("status") if matched_step else "current",
            "matched_era": matched_step.get("era") if matched_step else None,
        }

    def find_evolution_for_skills(
        self, skills: List[str]
    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Given a list of user skills (from CV), find all that have
        evolution chains — helping the user see career progression.

        Returns list of {skill, chain_id, current_term, career_insight, ...}
        """
        results = []
        seen_chains: Set[str] = set()
        for skill in skills:
            chain = self.resolve_evolution(skill)
            if chain and chain["chain_id"] not in seen_chains:
                seen_chains.add(chain["chain_id"])
                chain["user_skill"] = skill
                results.append(chain)
        return results

    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # 1c. SKILL SYNONYM RESOLUTION
    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def resolve_synonym(self, term: str) -> Optional[str]:
        """
        Resolve a term to its canonical form.

        "NLP" → "natural language processing"
        "organisation" → "organization"

        Returns canonical term, or None if not in synonym index.
        """
        return self._synonym_index.get(term.strip().lower())

    def canonicalise_skills(
        self, skills: List[str]
    ) -> Dict[str, str]:
        """
        Canonicalise a list of skills: resolve synonyms + abbreviations.

        Returns {original: canonical} for every skill that has a mapping.
        Unmapped skills are omitted from the result.
        """
        result: Dict[str, str] = {}
        for skill in skills:
            canonical = self.resolve_synonym(skill)
            if canonical and canonical != skill.strip().lower():
                result[skill] = canonical
        return result

    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # 1d. USER-LEARNED TERM PERSISTENCE
    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def learn_term(
        self,
        phrase: str,
        label: str,
        *,
        source: str = "user_cv",
        confidence: float = 0.6,
    ) -> bool:
        """
        Learn a new term discovered from a user's CV and persist it.

        The term is added to the runtime gazetteer immediately AND
        appended to user_learned_terms.json for persistence across
        restarts. Deduplicates automatically.

        Args:
            phrase:     multi-word expression ("blue hydrogen")
            label:      category label ("INDUSTRY_TERM")
            source:     origin ("user_cv", "admin", "ai_suggested")
            confidence: reliability score 0.0-1.0

        Returns:
            True if the term was new and added, False if already known.
        """
        norm = phrase.strip().lower()
        if not norm or len(norm.split()) < 2:
            return False
        if norm in self._custom_phrases:
            return False  # already known

        # Add to runtime
        self._custom_phrases[norm] = label
        self._phrase_matcher = None  # force rebuild
        self._entity_ruler = None
        self._nlp_for_matcher = None

        # Persist to disk
        if self._data_dir:
            fp = self._data_dir / "user_learned_terms.json"
            try:
                existing: list = []
                if fp.exists():
                    with open(fp, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                        existing = json.load(f)

                existing.append({
                    "phrase": norm,
                    "label": label,
                    "source": source,
                    "confidence": confidence,
                    "added_date": datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d"),
                })

                with open(fp, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                    json.dump(existing, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)

                logger.info("Learned new term: '%s' [%s] → persisted to disk", norm, label)
            except Exception as e:
                logger.warning("Failed to persist learned term '%s': %s", norm, e)

        return True

    def learn_terms_batch(
        self,
        terms: Dict[str, str],
        *,
        source: str = "user_cv",
        confidence: float = 0.6,
    ) -> int:
        """
        Learn multiple terms at once. Returns count of newly added terms.
        """
        added = 0
        for phrase, label in terms.items():
            if self.learn_term(phrase, label, source=source, confidence=confidence):
                added += 1
        return added

    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # 2. N-GRAM EXTRACTION
    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    @staticmethod
    def extract_ngrams(
        text: str,
        n: int = 2,
        *,
        lowercase: bool = True,
        remove_stopwords: bool = True,
    ) -> List[Tuple[str, ...]]:
        """
        Extract n-grams from text.

        Args:
            text: input text
            n:    2 for bigrams, 3 for trigrams, etc.
            lowercase: normalise to lowercase
            remove_stopwords: drop English stopwords from n-grams

        Returns:
            List of n-gram tuples: [("machine", "learning"), ("deep", "learning")]
        """
        # Simple tokenization (split on non-alphanumeric, keep dots for "Node.js")
        tokens = re.findall(r"[a-zA-Z0-9]+(?:\.[a-zA-Z0-9]+)?", text)
        if lowercase:
            tokens = [t.lower() for t in tokens]

        if remove_stopwords:
            stop = _get_stopwords()
            tokens = [t for t in tokens if t not in stop]

        ngrams = []
        for i in range(len(tokens) - n + 1):
            ngrams.append(tuple(tokens[i : i + n]))

        return ngrams

    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # 3. PMI / STATISTICAL COLLOCATION SCORING
    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def find_collocations(
        self,
        texts: List[str],
        *,
        n: int = 2,
        min_freq: int = 3,
        top_k: int = 200,
        method: str = "pmi",
    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Discover statistically significant word pairs/triples from a corpus.

        Uses NLTK's collocation finders with PMI, chi-squared, or likelihood ratio.

        Args:
            texts:    list of documents (each a string)
            n:        2 for bigrams, 3 for trigrams
            min_freq: minimum co-occurrence count to consider
            top_k:    return top K collocations
            method:   "pmi" | "chi_sq" | "likelihood_ratio" | "raw_freq"

        Returns:
            [{"ngram": ("machine", "learning"), "score": 8.2, "freq": 47, "method": "pmi"}]
        """
        try:
            import nltk
            from nltk.collocations import (
                BigramCollocationFinder,
                TrigramCollocationFinder,
                BigramAssocMeasures,
                TrigramAssocMeasures,
            )
        except ImportError:
            logger.error("NLTK required for collocation discovery: pip install nltk")
            return []

        # Ensure NLTK data is available
        _ensure_nltk_data()

        # Tokenize all texts
        all_tokens = []
        stop = _get_stopwords()
        for text in texts:
            tokens = re.findall(r"[a-zA-Z0-9]+(?:\.[a-zA-Z0-9]+)?", text.lower())
            tokens = [t for t in tokens if t not in stop and len(t) > 1]
            all_tokens.extend(tokens)

        if len(all_tokens) < n:
            return []

        # Build collocation finder
        if n == 2:
            finder = BigramCollocationFinder.from_words(all_tokens)
            measures = BigramAssocMeasures()
        elif n == 3:
            finder = TrigramCollocationFinder.from_words(all_tokens)
            measures = TrigramAssocMeasures()
        else:
            logger.warning("Only n=2 (bigram) and n=3 (trigram) supported, defaulting to 2")
            finder = BigramCollocationFinder.from_words(all_tokens)
            measures = BigramAssocMeasures()

        # Apply frequency filter
        finder.apply_freq_filter(min_freq)

        # Score with chosen method
        method_map = {
            "pmi": measures.pmi,
            "chi_sq": measures.chi_sq,
            "likelihood_ratio": measures.likelihood_ratio,
            "raw_freq": measures.raw_freq,
        }
        score_fn = method_map.get(method, measures.pmi)

        scored = finder.score_ngrams(score_fn)

        # Build results
        freq_dist = finder.ngram_fd
        results = []
        for ngram, score in scored[:top_k]:
            results.append({
                "ngram": ngram,
                "score": round(score, 4),
                "freq": freq_dist.get(ngram, 0),
                "method": method,
                "joined": "_".join(ngram),
            })

        # Cache learned collocations for use in tokenize_with_phrases
        for r in results:
            if n == 2:
                self._learned_collocations[r["ngram"]] = r["score"]

        return results

    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # 4. WINDOW CO-OCCURRENCE
    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    @staticmethod
    def window_cooccurrence(
        texts: List[str],
        *,
        window: int = 5,
        min_freq: int = 3,
        top_k: int = 100,
    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Find words that co-occur within a sliding window, even if not adjacent.

        Detects contextual pairs like ("Hydrogen", "Economy") when they appear
        on the same line/paragraph but not touching.

        Args:
            texts:    corpus of documents
            window:   number of tokens in the sliding window
            min_freq: minimum co-occurrence count
            top_k:    return top K pairs

        Returns:
            [{"pair": ("hydrogen", "economy"), "freq": 12,
              "pmi": 5.3, "window_size": 5}]
        """
        stop = _get_stopwords()

        # Count individual and pair frequencies
        word_freq: Counter = Counter()
        pair_freq: Counter = Counter()
        total_windows = 0

        for text in texts:
            tokens = re.findall(r"[a-zA-Z0-9]+(?:\.[a-zA-Z0-9]+)?", text.lower())
            tokens = [t for t in tokens if t not in stop and len(t) > 1]

            for i in range(len(tokens)):
                word_freq[tokens[i]] += 1
                win_end = min(i + window, len(tokens))
                total_windows += 1
                for j in range(i + 1, win_end):
                    pair = tuple(sorted([tokens[i], tokens[j]]))
                    pair_freq[pair] += 1

        if total_windows == 0:
            return []

        # Filter by minimum frequency
        frequent_pairs = {p: f for p, f in pair_freq.items() if f >= min_freq}

        # Calculate PMI for each pair
        n = sum(word_freq.values())
        results = []
        for pair, freq in frequent_pairs.items():
            w1, w2 = pair
            p_pair = freq / max(total_windows, 1)
            p_w1 = word_freq[w1] / max(n, 1)
            p_w2 = word_freq[w2] / max(n, 1)

            if p_w1 > 0 and p_w2 > 0 and p_pair > 0:
                pmi = math.log2(p_pair / (p_w1 * p_w2))
            else:
                pmi = 0.0

            results.append({
                "pair": pair,
                "freq": freq,
                "pmi": round(pmi, 4),
                "window_size": window,
            })

        # Sort by PMI descending
        results.sort(key=lambda x: x["pmi"], reverse=True)
        return results[:top_k]

    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # 5. PHRASE-AWARE TOKENIZATION (the main consumer-facing method)
    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def tokenize_with_phrases(
        self,
        text: str,
        *,
        lowercase: bool = True,
        lemmatize: bool = True,
        remove_stop: bool = True,
        pmi_threshold: float = 3.0,
        max_chars: int = 100_000,
    ) -> List[str]:
        """
        Tokenize text while preserving multi-word expressions as single tokens.

        Process:
          1. Run PhraseMatcher → replace matched spans with joined tokens
          2. Apply learned collocations (from find_collocations) above PMI threshold
          3. Standard spaCy tokenization + lemmatization on remaining text

        Example:
            "Senior machine learning engineer with AWS experience"
            → ["senior", "machine_learning", "engineer", "aws", "experience"]

        Args:
            text:          input text
            lowercase:     normalise to lowercase
            lemmatize:     return lemmas instead of surface forms
            remove_stop:   drop stopwords
            pmi_threshold: minimum PMI score to treat a bigram as a frozen phrase

        Returns:
            List of tokens (multi-word units joined with underscore)
        """
        text = text[:max_chars]

        # Step 1: Find domain phrases via PhraseMatcher
        try:
            self._ensure_matcher()
            phrase_matches = self.match_domain_phrases(text, max_chars=max_chars)
        except RuntimeError:
            phrase_matches = []

        # Build a replacement map: character spans → joined phrase
        replacements: List[Tuple[int, int, str]] = []
        for m in phrase_matches:
            joined = m["phrase"].lower().replace(" ", "_").replace("-", "_") if lowercase else m["phrase"].replace(" ", "_")
            replacements.append((m["start_char"], m["end_char"], joined))

        # Sort by start position (descending) so we can replace without shifting
        replacements.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)

        # Apply replacements to text (replace multi-word spans with joined tokens)
        modified = text
        for start, end, joined in replacements:
            modified = modified[:start] + joined + modified[end:]

        # Step 2: Tokenize the modified text
        tokens = re.findall(r"[a-zA-Z0-9_]+(?:\.[a-zA-Z0-9_]+)?", modified)
        if lowercase:
            tokens = [t.lower() for t in tokens]

        # Step 3: Apply learned collocations (bigram join above PMI threshold)
        if self._learned_collocations:
            merged = []
            i = 0
            while i < len(tokens):
                if i + 1 < len(tokens):
                    pair = (tokens[i], tokens[i + 1])
                    if self._learned_collocations.get(pair, 0) >= pmi_threshold:
                        merged.append(f"{tokens[i]}_{tokens[i + 1]}")
                        i += 2
                        continue
                merged.append(tokens[i])
                i += 1
            tokens = merged

        # Step 4: Optional stopword removal
        if remove_stop:
            stop = _get_stopwords()
            tokens = [t for t in tokens if t not in stop and len(t) > 1]

        return tokens

    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # 6. ABBREVIATION EXPANSION
    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def expand_abbreviations(
        self,
        text: str,
        *,
        custom_map: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
    ) -> str:
        """
        Expand known abbreviations in text.

        "Must know PLC and HMI" → "Must know PLC (programmable logic controller)
        and HMI (human machine interface)"

        Does NOT replace the abbreviation (it's often the preferred form) —
        instead appends the expansion in parentheses for downstream NLP.
        """
        abbrevs = dict(self._abbreviation_map)
        if custom_map:
            abbrevs.update(custom_map)

        result = text
        for abbr, expansion in abbrevs.items():
            # Match the abbreviation as a whole word (case-insensitive)
            pattern = rf"\b{re.escape(abbr)}\b(?!\s*\()"  # not already followed by (
            matches = list(re.finditer(pattern, result, re.IGNORECASE))
            # Replace in reverse order to preserve positions
            for m in reversed(matches):
                original = m.group()
                result = result[:m.end()] + f" ({expansion})" + result[m.end():]

        return result

    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # STATUS
    # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def status(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Report engine state."""
        return {
            "gazetteer_phrases": len(self._custom_phrases),
            "learned_collocations": len(self._learned_collocations),
            "phrase_matcher_loaded": self._phrase_matcher is not None,
            "entity_ruler_loaded": self._entity_ruler is not None,
            "abbreviation_map_size": len(self._abbreviation_map),
            "evolution_chains": len(self._evolution_chains),
            "synonym_entries": len(self._synonyms_raw),
            "synonym_index_size": len(self._synonym_index),
            "data_directory": str(self._data_dir) if self._data_dir else None,
            "source_files": self._source_stats,
            "available_methods": [
                "match_domain_phrases",
                "extract_domain_entities",
                "extract_ngrams",
                "find_collocations",
                "window_cooccurrence",
                "tokenize_with_phrases",
                "expand_abbreviations",
                "resolve_evolution",
                "find_evolution_for_skills",
                "resolve_synonym",
                "canonicalise_skills",
                "learn_term",
                "learn_terms_batch",
                "reload",
            ],
        }

    def reload(self) -> Dict[str, int]:
        """
        Reload all phrase data from disk.

        Call this after running mine_collocations.py or adding new JSON files
        to refresh the engine without restarting the process.

        Returns:
            source_stats — {filename: count} for each loaded file
        """
        self._data_dir = _resolve_data_dir()
        phrases, abbreviations, stats = _load_all_from_directory(self._data_dir)
        self._custom_phrases = phrases
        self._abbreviation_map = abbreviations
        self._source_stats = stats

        # Reload evolution chains
        self._evolution_chains = _load_evolution_chains(self._data_dir)
        self._evolution_index = _build_evolution_index(self._evolution_chains)

        # Reload synonyms
        self._synonyms_raw = _load_synonyms(self._data_dir)
        self._synonym_index = _build_synonym_index(self._synonyms_raw)

        # Reload user-learned terms
        user_terms = _load_user_learned(self._data_dir)
        if user_terms:
            self._custom_phrases.update(user_terms)
            stats["user_learned_terms"] = len(user_terms)

        # Force matcher rebuild on next use
        self._phrase_matcher = None
        self._entity_ruler = None
        self._nlp_for_matcher = None

        logger.info(
            "CollocationEngine: reloaded %d phrases + %d abbreviations + "
            "%d evolution chains + %d synonyms from %d files",
            len(phrases), len(abbreviations),
            len(self._evolution_chains), len(self._synonyms_raw),
            len(stats),
        )
        return stats


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# HELPERS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

_STOPWORDS: Optional[Set[str]] = None


def _get_stopwords() -> Set[str]:
    """Get English stopwords (NLTK or fallback)."""
    global _STOPWORDS
    if _STOPWORDS is not None:
        return _STOPWORDS

    try:
        _ensure_nltk_data()
        from nltk.corpus import stopwords
        _STOPWORDS = set(stopwords.words("english"))
    except Exception:
        # Minimal fallback
        _STOPWORDS = {
            "a", "an", "the", "and", "or", "but", "in", "on", "at", "to",
            "for", "of", "with", "by", "from", "is", "are", "was", "were",
            "be", "been", "being", "have", "has", "had", "do", "does", "did",
            "will", "would", "could", "should", "may", "might", "can",
            "this", "that", "these", "those", "i", "you", "he", "she", "it",
            "we", "they", "me", "him", "her", "us", "them", "my", "your",
            "his", "its", "our", "their", "what", "which", "who", "when",
            "where", "how", "not", "no", "nor", "if", "then", "than",
            "so", "as", "just", "about", "up", "out", "all", "also",
        }
    return _STOPWORDS


def _ensure_nltk_data() -> None:
    """Download required NLTK data if missing."""
    try:
        import nltk
        nltk.data.find("corpora/stopwords")
    except LookupError:
        import nltk
        nltk.download("stopwords", quiet=True)
    try:
        import nltk
        nltk.data.find("tokenizers/punkt_tab")
    except LookupError:
        import nltk
        nltk.download("punkt_tab", quiet=True)


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SINGLETON
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

collocation_engine = CollocationEngine()
